calls.title: Teachable <> Speravita: Organizations Review
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-09
ATTENDEES: Jennifer Roush (Speravita), Kristina Edelman, Liselle, Zach McCall, Lennie Zhu (Product), Sarah Dean (Product)
COMPANY: Speravita
STAGE: Evaluation / Product Deep Dive

---
SUMMARY
Product-focused call with Speravita (nonprofit serving pregnancy resource centers). They have 115 centers with three learning tracks (executive, medical, patient resources). Discussed org nesting, learning paths, and bundles as solutions for their multi-track, multi-center training model. Product team (Lennie + Sarah) provided direct input on roadmap.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Online training platform for pregnancy resource centers
Audience: Center staff across 3 tracks — executive directors, medical, patient resources
Business model: B2B nonprofit training sold to organizations
Content types: Recorded Zoom training, ebooks, live coaching webinars
Scale: 115 centers, 3-6 trained per cohort, minimum 3 participants per center

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Organizations: Need subgroups/nesting — centers with teams within them. Tags for regions and 'centers of distinction'
Learning paths: Three ordered learning tracks per center (exec, medical, patient resources)
Access duration: Time-limited access per enrollment (e.g., 2 months)
Reporting: Per-center, per-student progress tracking. Year-end aggregate reporting nice-to-have
Community: Interest in online community for cross-center engagement

---
PRODUCT ROADMAP SHARED
- Learning paths: Another team building in Q1, essentially what they need
- Org nesting: Lennie says Q2 for subgroup/nesting experience
- Access duration: End of March for product access duration feature
- Bundles: Available now as workaround for learning tracks

---
TIMELINE
June cohort training — need first day online by May 1st. Content partially ready (recorded Zooms, ebooks). Follow-up March 2nd at 10:30am.

---
ACTION ITEMS
Zach: Schedule March 2nd follow-up, keep team updated on feature releases
Speravita: Continue content preparation, define center structure needs

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KGG6JGCKPY1XHBX459A0TJK6

calls.title: Teachable Followup
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-05
ATTENDEES: Simon Smith, Sabine Lehner, Katrina Welch, Zach McCall, Lennie Zhu (Product)
COMPANY: Simon & Sabine (Leadership Coaching)
STAGE: Evaluation / Technical Scoping

---
SUMMARY
Technical scoping call for Australian leadership coaching program. Core challenge: automating 360-degree assessment delivery from Roche Martin and SciTec platforms 48 hours before each coaching session. Agreed this requires third-party automation outside Teachable. Zach to work with expert Tom on integration architecture.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Digitally transformed leadership coaching program
Audience: Corporate professionals (currently running 16 per cohort with Department of Defense)
Business model: B2B coaching — want to scale nationally, eventually internationally
Content types: Intro video, 360 assessment (Roche Martin), personality assessment (SciTec 15FQ+), coaching sessions

---
LEARNER FLOW
1. Group introduction (video or live webinar)
2. Complete 360-degree assessment via Roche Martin + SciTec assessment
3. Book coaching session via Calendly
4. 48 hours before session: receive assessment results automatically
5. Coaching sessions (multiple)
6. Upload development roadmap at end
7. Receive certificate

---
TECHNICAL CHALLENGE
- Assessment reports sit in Roche Martin and SciTec — need automation to pull and deliver
- Roche Martin operates on LearnWorlds platform (competitor)
- Both platforms send email notifications on completion
- Timed release (48 hours before coaching) not native to Teachable
- Solution likely requires: external automation (Zapier/custom), storage layer, calendar integration
- Lenny confirmed this would be a challenge on any platform — not Teachable-specific

---
BLOCKER
Simon: "We can't sign up to teachable with the costs that it has without that solution in place. There's no point. We've got to have that as a workable solution or at least a guarantee that it can happen before we proceed."

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach: Work with Tom (expert partner) on integration architecture. Come back with outline of how assessment delivery automation would work. Target: Monday/Tuesday email with preliminary plan.
Customer: May need to share sample assessment reports and platform access details for Tom.

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KG8DBQ8MRVVY6R18W0SQ99X7

CALL DATE: 2026-02-05
ATTENDEES: Drew Sarratore (BADM), Zach McCall, Liv
COMPANY: BADM
STAGE: Negotiation

---
SUMMARY
Contract negotiation call with BADM, a nonprofit. Going with Unlimited plan for the account manager. Discussed white glove onboarding vs account manager strategy support. Drew prefers building institutional knowledge with account manager first, may add white glove later.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Online learning platform for nonprofit
Audience: Their community/learners
Business model: Nonprofit — educational, pedagogical background
Content types: Deep pedagogical content, specific learning experiences

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Unlimited
Discounts offered: Nonprofit ($500/yr off) + multi-year ($500/yr off for 2 or 3 year)
Contract length discussed: 1, 2, or 3 year options (2 and 3 year same price)
White glove: $2K-6K range, can add later but subsidized if upfront

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Already decided on Unlimited plan
- Ready to receive contract options
- Asking about next steps and timeline

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Send 1-year and 2/3-year contract options with nonprofit discount
- Include white glove onboarding pricing
- Get basic business info for agreement

Customer:
- Review contract options
- Discuss white glove vs account manager with Liv and Michelle
- Drew out of office tomorrow and Monday

calls.attendees: Hui Ching LEE (LTA), Kai Shan TEO (LTA)
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-05
ATTENDEES: Hui Ching LEE (LTA), Kai Shan TEO (LTA), Zach McCall
COMPANY: Land Transport Authority (LTA) Singapore
STAGE: Followup / Pricing Discussion

---
SUMMARY
Second call with Singapore Land Transport Authority. Government agency running free training for vulnerable commuters (seniors, wheelchair users). Focus on Mandarin translation, quiz/certificate flow, pricing packages. Very basic use case — primarily need video courses translated to Mandarin with quiz assessment and e-certificate.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Public safety training for vulnerable commuters in Singapore
Audience: Senior citizens and commuters — Mandarin-speaking, not tech-savvy
Business model: Free public service (government funded, no revenue)
Content types: Video courses with Mandarin captions, quizzes, e-certificates
Scale: Individual enrollments (not organizations)

---
KEY REQUIREMENTS
Translation: All student-facing content in Mandarin (admin stays English)
Quiz: 80% passing grade required for e-certificate
Certificate: Must be in Chinese (custom HTML)
Reporting: CSV export of student progress and quiz results
Scheduling: Ability to schedule course publish dates
Voiceover: Asked but not available — videos use captions only

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Base plan: $6,500/year
Discount possibilities: Government/nonprofit discount + multi-year (2 or 3 year)
Payment: Annual, can do ACH or wire transfer
Timeline: Q2 launch target

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach: Check for government/nonprofit discount, send 1/2/3-year pricing packages
LTA: Review packages, confirm Q2 launch timeline

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KGK0S3TVKT6FZ6HN4TWGHJBH

calls.title: Dot Compliance <> Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-04
ATTENDEES: Ibrahim Haleem Khan (Dot Compliance), Bryan Aho (Dot Compliance), Oghenetejiri Kofi (Security), Stormy Willard (Dot Compliance), Zach McCall
COMPANY: Dot Compliance
STAGE: Initial Evaluation / Technical Testing

---
SUMMARY
First technical deep-dive with Dot Compliance. Ibrahim and team tested the platform hands-on during the call — discovered video scrub prevention works (90% enforcement prevents skipping) but needs batch processing time. Explored custom checkout fields for DOT numbers, guest checkout, affiliate program, cart/multi-item checkout, DRM protections, and API integration. Bryan and Ibrahim deeply engaged with platform testing.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Compliance training platform for commercial drivers (CDL, HAZMAT, DOT)
Audience: Trucking companies, fleet operators, individual drivers
Business model: B2B and B2C — sell training courses, sometimes multi-seat purchases
Content types: Compliance video courses with quizzes
Scale: Companies purchasing 1-10+ seats per training

---
TECHNICAL TESTING RESULTS
- Video scrub prevention: Works at 90% — can drag bar but 'Complete' button stays disabled until 90% watched
- Custom checkout fields: Can add 3 custom fields (DOT number, Agent ID, etc.) — feature being turned on for customers
- Guest checkout: Works when course has a price (free courses skip checkout)
- Affiliate program: Ibrahim set up and tested, confirmed working
- Cart/multi-item: Not available yet, big checkout update coming
- DRM: Downloads can be disabled, video player has Hotmart protections
- Analytics: GA4 and HotJar integration available natively

---
SECURITY QUESTIONS (Kofi)
- PCI compliance for credit card storage — Zach to confirm
- Bot prevention — OTP via email available (not SSO level)
- API changes conversation — security team needs full PCI flow details

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach: Run all questions through product team in morning. Confirm PCI details, cart/multi-item timeline, custom field API exposure. Add admin seats for team.
Customer: Continue platform testing. Ibrahim to compile any additional questions.

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KGN4PT9YVSHZJE86J8THBX0W

calls.title: Teachable Followup
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-03
ATTENDEES: Simon Davey, Zach McCall
COMPANY: Simon Davey (Australian Training Company)
STAGE: Followup / Technical Scoping

---
SUMMARY
Short followup with Simon Davey, an Australian training company. They love the platform but their developers say API integration for custom frontend is a significant build. 80% of business is in-person training — online is secondary but needs seamless frontend. Agreed Zach will collect developer pain points and route to product team / expert partners.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Custom-branded frontend with Teachable as headless backend
Audience: Corporate training clients (80% in-person, 20% online)
Business model: B2B training — mix of in-person and online
Content types: In-person manuals + online courses, exam processing
Key requirement: Headless/API design — seamless website-to-Teachable experience

---
BLOCKER
Developer team says API integration is still a very significant dev outlay. Need to understand what Teachable can help with vs. what requires custom build.

---
NEXT STEPS
Simon: Get dev team (Paul's team) to document specific API limitations and send to Zach
Zach: Route to product team, check for existing solutions or upcoming features. Connect with expert if needed. Possibly set up technical call between dev teams.

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KG0TD79BR1B1FN8TDWRPMPAS

calls.title: Teachable <> Dot Compliance Followup
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-02
ATTENDEES: Ibrahim Haleem Khan (Dot Compliance), Brian (Dot Compliance), Zach McCall
COMPANY: Dot Compliance
STAGE: Followup / Evaluation

---
SUMMARY
Second followup with Dot Compliance. They've uploaded videos to trial, playing around with the platform. Deep discussion on phased integration: MVP with built-in checkout first, then WordPress/WooCommerce checkout, then Salesforce integration (July/August). Heavy focus on API capabilities, organization nesting for supervisor roles, and certificate validation.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Compliance training for commercial drivers (HAZMAT, DOT regulations)
Audience: Companies with fleets of drivers; supervisors manage training assignments
Business model: B2B — sell training seats to companies, recurring annual certification
Content types: Compliance video courses with knowledge verification
Scale expectations: Thousands of customers, each potentially an organization with many drivers

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Need total cost of ownership including integration engineering
Authority: Ibrahim (technical lead), Brian (project management), Abraham (web/checkout)
Need: Compliance training platform with phased integration into existing systems
Timeline: Want MVP ASAP; Salesforce integration by July/August; phased rollout

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Salesforce (5 months), WordPress/WooCommerce checkout, API for user enrollment/entitlements
Reporting needs: Completion tracking per organization, supervisor-level visibility
Payments / checkout: MVP using Teachable built-in, then migrate to own WordPress/WooCommerce checkout
Admin or seat management: Unique seats per user, organization-level enrollment, supervisor roles
Special workflows: Phased migration, OTP login for MVP, certificate validation with serial numbers and expiration dates

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Already uploaded all video content to trial
- Very detailed technical questions about integration
- Discussing implementation timeline actively
- Asked about engineering support pricing

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Capacity crunch on their engineering team
- Need total cost of ownership before committing
- Multiple parallel initiatives (Salesforce, new website, Teachable) competing for resources
- Certificate validation/verification needs may exceed current capabilities

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Video watching enforcement feature hard to find in UI
- Organization nesting with supervisors is critical — Zach confirmed end of Q1
- Certificate verification (QR code, online validation, expiration) not fully supported
- Email notifications within organizations needed

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Send API documentation with user creation/enrollment examples
- Set up scoping call with integration engineering expert
- Send Teachable training course access to Ibrahim's team

Customer:
- Continue platform evaluation in trial
- Send compiled questions list to Zach
- Ibrahim to determine priority of integration phases

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KFXJC3VJV91SFF5PRNCY3XBY

calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-11
ATTENDEES: Anne Blocker (ESI), Zach McCall, Jonathan Corvin-Blackburn (Product Designer, Learning Experience team)
COMPANY: ESI (Eating Smart International / nutrition education nonprofit)
STAGE: Retention / Feature Review

---
SUMMARY
Retention call with long-time Teachable customer (since ~2017-2019). Currently on two platforms — Teachable and Learn Worlds. Anne gave extensive feedback on quiz limitations, certificate customization, and learning path needs. Jonathan (product designer) confirmed these are top 3 priorities for his team. Anne's org did $18K in revenue on Teachable last year.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: CE credit courses for nutrition/dietetics professionals
Audience: Nutritionists, dietitians — need continuing education credits from Academy of Nutrition & Dietetics
Business model: Small nonprofit, mostly volunteers, growing online revenue
Content types: Webinar recordings, self-studies, quizzes, evaluations, certificate programs
Scale: Individual learners + occasional organization bulk purchases

---
QUIZ FEATURE REQUESTS
- Question bank: Reuse questions across multiple quizzes
- CSV import/export: Edit questions in spreadsheet, re-upload
- New question types: True/false, matching, star rating, thumbs up/down, weighted questions
- Quiz analytics: Which questions people miss, which wrong answers they pick
- Currently forced to use SurveyMonkey for evaluations because quiz is too limited

---
CERTIFICATE REQUESTS
- Custom fields: CE credit hours, license number auto-fill
- Academy format compliance: Specific fields required by accrediting body
- Student data auto-fill: License/registration numbers entered at enrollment appear on certificate
- Jonathan has internally proposed similar onboarding survey → certificate auto-fill feature

---
LEARNING PATH INTEREST
- Group 3 webinars as ordered course with certificate at end
- Enforce completion order across courses
- Certificate program: multiple courses → final PowerPoint upload → grading → certificate

---
WHAT THEY LOVE ABOUT TEACHABLE
- Discussion chats (pre-loaded prompts, instructor responses, email backup)
- Digital downloads and video sales (segmented, clean layout)
- Stripe Tax included (saves $1,200/year vs Learn Worlds)
- Mobile app with white label option
- Coupons/discounts easy to create
- Transparent pricing (no hidden fees like Learn Worlds)

---
WHAT THEY DISLIKE / MISSING
- Quiz is basic (only multiple choice)
- Certificate customization limited
- Reporting for QuickBooks reconciliation difficult
- Course navigation feels clunky (jumping between videos and handouts)
- Coupon code field hard for new customers to find
- Refund button doesn't give visual confirmation → caused triple refund
- No in-course upsell/cross-sell (must leave to purchase another course)
- Stripe Express limits: can't reverse accidental refunds directly

---
COMPETITIVE CONTEXT
- Learn Worlds: Better quiz tools (CSV import, question types), interactive video, website builder
- Learn Worlds downsides: No real mobile app ($3,600/yr extra), no Stripe Tax ($1,200/yr), hidden costs, poor discussion chat
- Anne reluctant to leave Teachable — considering staying if improvements come

---
PRODUCT ROADMAP SHARED
Jonathan (Learning Experience team):
- Learning paths: In development now (Q1)
- Quizzes: New question types + CSV import/export starting ~April
- Certificates: Improvements planned for summer
- File upload block exists (open-ended) but no grading workflow yet

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach: Follow up on pricing, connect Anne with features she may not know about (upsell blocks, order bumps, Zoom integration)
Jonathan: Report feedback to team, continue learning paths development

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KH4P5ZAKDS06QNRRA8PWX3ZX

calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-13
ATTENDEES: Stephanie Manly, Candice K
COMPANY: Red Rover
STAGE: Demo

---
SUMMARY
Red Rover is a software company serving school districts. They need an LMS for two use cases: (1) training customer super users (school district admins) on how to use Red Rover, and (2) internal staff onboarding. Jerome ran the initial discovery; this was the follow-up demo with Zach.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Customer training for school district super users + internal staff onboarding
Audience: 1-3 super users per school district, plus internal new hires
Business model: Free training for existing customers (no commerce needed)
Content types: Videos, quizzes, checklists, interactive content
Scale expectations: Small-medium user base, 20 admins sufficient

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Asked about pricing, no pushback on $6,500/year base
Authority: Stephanie appears to be decision-maker, evaluating multiple LMS platforms
Need: Strong — currently no way to track if customers complete training
Timeline: Want super user course built by June for fall school year onboarding

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Zendesk (help center), Red Rover platform (API for enrollment + completion tracking), Microsoft SSO for internal staff
Reporting needs: Per-group, per-student, per-course completion tracking; flag completions back in Red Rover
Payments / checkout: Not needed — free training for existing customers
Admin or seat management: ~20 admins sufficient, organizations for school district grouping
Special workflows or constraints: Persistent checklists for task completion, interactive image hotspots, matching/sorting quiz types

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Asked for sandbox access to play around
- Discussed specific timeline (June for fall school year)
- Narrowing down options, wants follow-up next week
- Said 'That actually is kind of cool' about embedded site option

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Quiz types limited to multiple choice currently — they need matching, sorting, interactive hotspots
- No native checklist functionality
- Need API/technical setup help but don't have deep technical expertise internally
- Evaluating multiple LMS platforms

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Need matching, sorting, and interactive image hotspot quiz types
- Need persistent checklist/to-do functionality within lessons
- Need comment moderation/approval process

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Base plan at $6,500/year
Discounts offered: None mentioned
Contract length discussed: Annual
Constraints or approvals needed: Need to narrow down options, internal discussion with Candice

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Send pricing structure and add-on menu
- Set up sandbox account
- Give access to Teachable U for student experience
- Follow up next week

Customer:
- Internal discussion to narrow down LMS options
- Explore sandbox account

Scheduled:
- Follow-up next week

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
Stephanie is the primary contact and decision-driver. Candice is more technical/detail-oriented (came from the classroom). They're in early evaluation phase comparing multiple LMS platforms. Jerome ran the initial discovery call. They're not highly technical but have technical people on their team who could handle API setup.

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KH9S8XRSHHFYQCWWMT9GDNQW

calls.title: Connect With Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-09
ATTENDEES: Shannon, Tony K
COMPANY: Transcend Analytics
STAGE: Demo

---
SUMMARY
Transcend Analytics is a consulting firm for home care and hospice agencies. They want to build a turnkey subscription product to sell training courses and provide a knowledge base/wiki for just-in-time learning. They're training salespeople at home care agencies. Evaluating ~12 LMS platforms. Have specific AI (Microsoft Copilot) and M365 integration requirements. Jerome ran the initial discovery.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Subscription-based training platform for home care/hospice industry
Audience: Salespeople at home care/hospice agencies (clients pay for access)
Business model: Paid subscriptions + coupon codes for large clients under SOW
Content types: Video courses, wiki/resource library, AI tutor
Scale expectations: Multiple agencies, on-demand + structured learning

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: No pushback on $10K base; most land $15-25K range mentioned
Authority: Tony and Shannon appear to be co-decision makers
Need: Strong — evaluating 12 platforms, specific technical requirements
Timeline: Prototype by end of April

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Microsoft 365 / Copilot API (AI tutor hosted in their environment), Zapier (existing), SCORM compliance (needed for avatar uploads)
Reporting needs: Email push reports for business partners, CSV export, custom dashboards
Payments / checkout: Subscription model + coupon codes for SOW clients + credit card for on-demand
Admin or seat management: Organization-level access control per agency
Special workflows or constraints: Non-linear learning (wiki/knowledge base), AI tutor powered by their own Copilot agent

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Detailed technical questions about API and embedding
- Already have Zapier infrastructure
- Specific prototype deadline (end of April)
- Asked about pricing and development timing

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Wiki/knowledge base is not Teachable's core strength (Zach recommended Confluence)
- SCORM compliance not available until beginning of Q3
- Need API help to connect Copilot to Teachable
- Evaluating 12 other platforms

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Need SCORM compliance for avatar content uploads
- Need wiki/knowledge base for non-linear just-in-time learning
- Want email push reports (not just manual CSV export)

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Base at $10,000/year, most land $15-25K with custom work
Discounts offered: None mentioned
Contract length discussed: Annual
Constraints or approvals needed: Need to scope custom AI/M365 integration work

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Recap email with requirements list
- Connect them with Tom (technical expert) for scoping
- Get scope and cost estimate for custom work

Customer:
- Review requirements list
- Confirm custom build needs

Scheduled:
- Follow-up after scoping

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
Tony is more technical/strategic, Shannon handles operations. They have an instructional designer helping them build content. Tony had to leave early. They're serious evaluators with a clear timeline and budget. The wiki/knowledge base need is a potential gap — Zach was transparent that Teachable isn't the best fit for that piece.

---

calls.title: Connect with Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-06
ATTENDEES: Luke Easley
COMPANY: Biblical Counseling Organization
STAGE: Demo

---
SUMMARY
Luke runs a virtual biblical counseling program with ~25 contracted counselors and ~200 students. Currently using Fiducare (small, limited platform) for 10+ years. Looking for a platform where counselors have their own login, can see assigned students, deliver courses, and conduct one-on-one video sessions. They have extensive printed resources to digitize into courses. Jerome ran the initial discovery.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Virtual counseling program platform with courses, assignments, and live sessions
Audience: ~200 counselees assigned to ~25 counselors
Business model: Students pay upfront or in 3 installments for 4-month program
Content types: Digitized printed resources (PDFs, workbooks), video sessions, weekly assignments
Scale expectations: 200 students, 25 counselors, several admins

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Not discussed directly, asked about trial access
Authority: Luke appears to be primary decision-maker
Need: Strong — outgrowing current platform after 10+ years
Timeline: Up and running in ~6 months

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Zapier for connecting existing systems, Calendly for scheduling, video conferencing (Teams likely)
Reporting needs: Per-counselor student progress, quiz scores
Payments / checkout: Full payment or 3 installments, Klarna integration discussed
Admin or seat management: Custom admin permissions for counselors, student-counselor assignment
Special workflows or constraints: White label platform + mobile app, custom role labels ("counselor" not "author"), private counselor-student communities, weekly drip content over 4 months

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Asked for trial access
- Said "this could work" multiple times
- Scheduled follow-up for Feb 17 at 2:30 PM ET
- Will send 3-4 page requirements document
- Wants white label app and platform

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Video coaching sessions not natively built into Teachable (requires external tool)
- "Author" terminology doesn't fit their counseling model
- Counselors need very limited control (just assigned students), not full author ecosystem
- Currently evaluating other options

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Need ability to rename "author" role to custom term (e.g., "counselor")
- Need native one-on-one video sessions within the platform
- Need student-counselor assignment in the back end
- Want private counselor-student messaging where students can't see other students

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Not specifically quoted
Discounts offered: Mentioned subsidized pricing for upfront custom work purchases
Contract length discussed: Not specified
Constraints or approvals needed: Has a written requirements document to share

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Send recap email
- Send menu of technical add-ons (white label, custom dashboard, etc.)
- Send trial/sandbox access
- Send article on drip settings
- Meeting Feb 17 at 2:30 PM ET

Customer:
- Send 3-4 page requirements document
- Review recap and add-on menu
- Try sandbox

Scheduled:
- Feb 17 at 2:30 PM ET follow-up

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
Jerome described Luke as "dry" personality-wise. Religious organization. Luke is practical and focused on the specific workflows of their counseling program. He's less concerned about the coaching side branding and more about the student-facing white label experience. Has been on Fiducare for 10+ years so this is a big platform migration.

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KGSVCEQV93YVSD8NTK04QH7B

calls.title: Connect With Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-06
ATTENDEES: Camilla, Scott Kaplan
COMPANY: New York Epoxy
STAGE: Demo

---
SUMMARY
New York Epoxy is an epoxy flooring company based in Florida. They do in-person trainings and want to launch an online course extension to reach people who can't travel. Camilla is the owner; Scott Kaplan is a business advisor with a home services background. They have ~30 videos (3-15 min each) ready. They want a seamless launch with quizzing and certificates. Leaning toward the Growth self-serve plan.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Launch online training course for epoxy flooring installation
Audience: Blue collar tradespeople, home services professionals adding epoxy to their skill set
Business model: Paid course (individuals + potentially businesses buying seats)
Content types: ~30 videos covering floor prep, systems, application, mixing chemicals
Scale expectations: Starting small, one course, growth potential

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Looking at Growth plan at $309/month; price-sensitive
Authority: Camilla is the decision-maker, Scott advises
Need: Moderate — content is ready, just needs a platform
Timeline: Very short runway, wants to launch ASAP (within a week)

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: None specified
Reporting needs: Course completion tracking, content drop-off analysis
Payments / checkout: Credit card, potentially coupons for single use
Admin or seat management: Basic for now, organizations feature interesting for B2B later
Special workflows or constraints: Simple UX critical — audience may be first-time online learners

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Content already ready (~30 videos)
- Leaning toward Growth plan
- Asked about onboarding timeline ("can we do it in one week?")
- Said "I'm sure this will be fine" and "that's what I originally was leaning towards"
- Was previously going to use Wap (competitor) but decided against it

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Price-sensitive: asked if there's a cheaper plan since they only need one course
- May go self-serve without premium plan (no account manager)
- Scott concerned about support SLA (8-hour ticket response for self-serve)

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Growth ($309/month self-serve) vs Premium ($6,000/year)
Discounts offered: Multi-year saves $500; strategy tip to start on Starter then upgrade
Contract length discussed: Monthly self-serve vs annual premium
Constraints or approvals needed: Camilla deciding between Growth and Advanced

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Send recap email
- Send links to home services companies built on Teachable (HVAC, cement)
- Available if they decide to go Premium

Customer:
- Decide on Growth vs Advanced plan
- Upload content and launch

Scheduled:
- None explicitly — self-serve signup expected

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
Scott is a sharp advisor who recently exited a home services company. He's thinking about the audience (tradespeople) and UX simplification. Camilla is straightforward and action-oriented. Jerome ran the initial discovery with Scott in December. They were considering Wap (School-like platform) before this. Low complexity deal but potential for growth if they sell to businesses later.

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KGSMR21SY88YC3P4JE88BEYF

calls.title: Teachable <> Unite Health
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-17
ATTENDEES: Sarah Todd (Unite Health, Owner), Simon Davey (Unite Health, Business Advisor), Fergus Haywood (Unite Health, Developer), Chacier Warhurst (Teachable, Senior PM), Zach McCall (Teachable)
COMPANY: Unite Health
STAGE: Technical Evaluation / API Deep Dive

---
SUMMARY
Technical deep dive with Unite Health (Australian Pilates instructor certification company). They want a custom e-commerce marketing site with Teachable powering courses and checkout. Discussed two approaches: traditional site with Teachable integration vs headless. Chacier (PM) detailed current API limitations (courses-only) and upcoming endpoints (all product types, user sessions, comments) expected by end of Q1. HubSpot integration confirmed as native (built by HubSpot team). SSO discussed as potential need. They're at the 'pointy end' of platform selection.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Custom e-commerce + LMS website for Pilates instructor certification
Audience: Fitness professionals seeking Pilates instructor certification
Business model: ~80% in-person training, expanding online. Three offerings: online, Zoom, in-person
Content types: Video courses, quizzes/exams, file upload assignments
Scale: 14-year-old business, working with NZ dev team (New Territory) + design agency

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
API/Headless: Need course surfacing on custom front-end, considering headless vs traditional integration
SSO: Potential need, no IDP selected yet. Chacier confirmed custom SSO has been done before
Payment plans: Automated installment plans through Stripe confirmed
HubSpot: Native integration exists, webhook data flows seamlessly
Quizzes: Multiple choice + file upload for graded assignments, instructor marking access needed
Physical products: Payment works but no fulfillment
Custom design: External design agency, may build on Teachable site builder with JS overrides

---
ROADMAP SHARED
- Full product type API endpoints: end of Q1 2026
- User session management endpoints: in current work stream
- Detailed quiz/assessment API endpoints: in current work stream
- Comments API: in current work stream

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach: Connect with Teachable experts for site build scoping, get back to Simon with recommendations
Unite Health: Design agency to create website concept, dev team to finalize build approach

---
FEATURE REQUESTS
- Headless / API Access (~04:00) - "Another kind of idea was going completely headless"
- SSO / SAML (~08:00) - "The single sign on stuff, that was kind of one of the limitations"
- Quiz Question Types (~14:00) - "Multiple choice exam and video content, can they all be uploaded?"
- File Upload & Grading (~15:00) - "Marking wise, can that be sent to certain instructors?"
- Checkout / Sales Page UX (~12:00) - "User journey from seeing a course through to purchase being seamless"
- Third-party Integration (~22:00) - "Wondering if you guys are completely integrated with HubSpot"
- Custom Themes (~24:00) - "Design agency coming up with the design of the website"
---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KH1GC7T9PKQWFANY68TQCB71

calls.title: Connect with Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-17
ATTENDEES: Charlie Durkin (Prospect, Melbourne AU), Jerome Olaloye (Teachable, BDR)
COMPANY: Coxswain Training Co. (rowing education — exact company name not stated)
STAGE: Discovery / Qualification — Demo scheduled

---
SUMMARY
Short BDR discovery call with Charlie Durkin, who runs a learning platform for rowing programs that trains coxswains (coxes). They deliver video-based courses to schools and universities worldwide, featuring Olympic coxes and coaches as instructors. Currently on Kajabi but actively looking to switch — Kajabi is functional but not engaging enough for their 14-23 year-old student audience. Primary pain point is learner engagement and UX: they want gamification, interactive quizzes, guided course journeys, and better content presentation. Charlie explicitly stated UX is the deal maker/breaker. Timeline is ASAP if the right platform is found. Jerome qualified the opportunity and booked a demo call with Zach McCall for Thursday at 9:30am Melbourne time.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Engaging online learning platform for coxswain (rowing) training
Audience: Students ages 14-23 at schools and universities globally
Content: High-production video lessons taught by Olympic coxes and coaches, plus quizzes
Business model: Course delivery to rowing programs at educational institutions
Current platform: Kajabi (looking to migrate)

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Gamification: Needs interactive, gamified elements to engage young learners
Quiz interactivity: Current quiz experience is too basic (click quiz, close quiz)
Guided learning path: Wants structured course journey, not just a list of videos
Aesthetic design: Needs visually appealing, modern UX for Gen Z audience
Video hosting: Must host high-quality video content (currently working well on Kajabi)

---
NEXT STEPS
Demo call with Zach McCall: Thursday 2026-02-20 at 9:30am Melbourne time (AEDT)
Focus: Learner-facing UX demo — Charlie explicitly requested the demo focus on user experience, not admin/backend
Jerome to pass notes to Zach with UX as the priority agenda item

---
FEATURE REQUESTS
- Gamification & Interactive Learning (~01:15) — "A platform that's a little bit more interactive, potentially gamified"
- Learner UX / Course Journey (~02:00) — "Something more guided through, aesthetically pleasing, takes the user on a journey"
- Interactive Quizzes (~02:15) — "Making the quizzes a bit more interactive"
- Content Presentation & Layout (~01:45) — "Presenting the information in a better way" (not just a list of videos)

---

calls.title: Teachable <> Pure Life Ministries
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-17
ATTENDEES: Zach McCall (Teachable), Luke Easley (Teachable), Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)
COMPANY: Pure Life Ministries
STAGE: Discovery / Followup (Trial Extension)

---
SUMMARY
Pure Life Ministries is a nonprofit/ministry organization building a 16-week counseling program called "Overcomers at Home" on Teachable. They are on an extended trial and the prospect has been actively building out the course structure. The call focused on how to integrate one-on-one counseling sessions (via video) into the course flow, handle coach-specific document distribution, and manage student assignment uploads. The prospect needs to present a complete workflow to their leadership team before moving forward with a purchase.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Deliver a structured 16-week counseling/course program ("Overcomers at Home") combining digital curriculum with weekly one-on-one video counseling sessions
Audience: Program participants seeking counseling/recovery support through the ministry
Business model: Bundled course + coaching (students purchase together); coaches paid per-session monthly outside of Teachable
Content types: Video lessons, reading assignments with physical books, downloadable handouts/homework, open-ended question uploads (photos of handwritten notes), one-on-one video counseling sessions
Scale expectations: Multiple coaches with assigned students; ~50 different supplemental documents across coaches

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Not discussed
Authority: Prospect must present to leadership team for approval before moving forward
Need: Strong — currently building out the program, deeply engaged in trial
Timeline: Dependent on leadership approval; actively building during trial period

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Calendly for coach booking, Zoom or Microsoft Teams for video sessions
Reporting needs: Coaches need to see individual student submissions (open-ended question reports)
Payments / checkout: Bundle packaging (course + coaching together); coach compensation handled externally (per-session monthly pay)
Admin or seat management: Multiple coaches as authors who can customize course content per student; coach-specific document distribution
Special workflows or constraints: Students must follow structured weekly progression; coaches assign supplemental documents outside the standard course structure; organization wants to avoid email for document distribution

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Actively building course content during extended trial
- Has week one fully set up with curriculum sections
- Asking detailed implementation questions (not just browsing)
- Needs to present to leadership — indicating organizational buy-in process is underway

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Coaching/counseling flow feels disjointed — prospect expected sessions to be embedded within the course rather than managed separately through Calendly
- Document distribution model (coach-specific handouts to specific students) doesn't map cleanly to Teachable's product/bundle model
- Multiple moving pieces and stakeholders — prospect expressed this is complex with "a lot of people involved"
- No account manager access until purchase — prospect needs hands-on guidance to build the workflow for leadership presentation
- Prospect wants to avoid email but Teachable's current options for targeted document delivery may push them toward email anyway

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Wants one-on-one video counseling embedded directly in the course flow (not external booking)
- Needs per-student document assignment capability for coaches
- Wants coach-specific digital downloads visible only to that coach's students
- Desires in-platform messaging/chat to send materials to students

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Respond to follow-up questions via email as the prospect continues building

Customer:
- Continue building out the course structure
- Test the coaching/bundle workflow
- Present the complete workflow to leadership team for approval

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
The prospect is hands-on and technically engaged — they were actively screen-sharing their Teachable build during the call. The organization has a fairly complex use case combining structured curriculum with personalized counseling, which doesn't perfectly map to Teachable's standard course + coaching model. The call ended abruptly when Zach had another meeting, leaving several questions unresolved (particularly around coach-specific document distribution). The prospect seems committed but needs clearer answers on the coaching and document workflow before they can get leadership buy-in. This is a nonprofit/ministry, so pricing sensitivity may be a factor.

---
FEATURE REQUESTS
- Structured Multi-Week Course (~02:30) - "So basically we have our Overcomers at Home program is what we call it, and it's a 16 week program."
- Integrated One-on-One Coaching Sessions (~03:00) - "And then each week they'll also have a counseling session which will be one on one video."
- In-Course Zoom Integration (~03:20) - "I guess I could integrate Zoom into here and add that as part of the section here."
- Coaching Embedded in Course Flow (~05:00) - "So they would have to go to something totally different to do their session."
- Student Onboarding Workflow (~05:30) - "I'm trying to just think from intake, having the student on board into this course."
- Course + Coaching Bundle (~09:30) - "We're going to have to put coaching in here. That feature in here."
- Student File Upload for Assignments (~14:00) - "And they have to answer questions before they can upload a picture of."
- Coach View of Student Submissions (~15:00) - "If I'm a coach then I would only see student."
- Coach-Assigned Custom Documents (~16:00) - "If I'm a coach and I have a specific item that may not necessarily be in the structure of the course."
- Per-Student Course Customization by Coach (~17:00) - "Can a coach go into a specific lesson of the course and modify that for their specific student?"
- Microsoft Teams Integration (~17:30) - "And you said there was Microsoft Teams integration or something."
- Calendly Integration for Coaching Booking (~18:30) - "So do we need to purchase a Calendly account or."
- In-Platform Messaging to Students (~20:00) - "Is there another way to send it to the student through teachable? Is it like a chat type thing?"
- Coach-Specific Digital Downloads (~21:00) - "We'll probably have 50 different digital downloads but we don't want every student to see those."
- Avoid Email for Document Distribution (~22:00) - "We're trying to avoid email."

---

quotes.text: A lot of times there's a lot. A lot of moving pieces into this where, you know, there's a lot of people involved.

questions_asked.question: What is the best way to integrate one-on-one counseling sessions into the course?
questions_asked.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

questions_asked.question: I guess I could integrate Zoom into here and add that as part of the section here. Because then if the student is on week one, could they click into that zoom thing?
questions_asked.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

questions_asked.question: So they would have to go to something totally different to do their session?
questions_asked.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

questions_asked.question: So if I wanted to set up like a test student or make myself a test student and have a test coach and all that stuff, what's the best way to do that?
questions_asked.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

questions_asked.question: Where does this go when it's uploaded? Does the coach see the submit?
questions_asked.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

questions_asked.question: If I'm a coach and I have a specific item that may not necessarily be in the structure of the course that we built, there's like a specific document, homework or handout, something like that. How would I assign that to a student?
questions_asked.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

questions_asked.question: Can a coach go into a specific lesson of the course and modify that for their specific student?
questions_asked.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

questions_asked.question: And you said there was Microsoft Teams integration or something?
questions_asked.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

questions_asked.question: So do we need to purchase a Calendly account?
questions_asked.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

questions_asked.question: But that would take the student outside of Teachable if they're trying to book things. Correct?
questions_asked.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

questions_asked.question: Is there another way to send it to the student through Teachable? Is it like a chat type thing or something?
questions_asked.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

objections.objection: Coaching sessions require leaving the course experience (disjointed UX)
objections.quote: So they would have to go to something totally different to do their session.
objections.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

objections.objection: Need to present full workflow to leadership before committing
objections.quote: I have to present to our leadership team exactly how it's all going to work before we the forward.
objections.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

objections.objection: Calendly booking takes students outside Teachable
objections.quote: But that would pick the student outside of teachable if they're trying to book things. Correct.
objections.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

objections.objection: Coach-specific document distribution doesn't fit product model
objections.quote: We'll probably have 50 different digital downloads, I guess, but we don't want every student to see those.
objections.speaker: Prospect (Pure Life Ministries)

barriers_to_adoption: Leadership team approval required before purchase
Complex use case with multiple coaches and per-student customization
Coaching workflow feels disjointed from course experience
Coach-specific document distribution not natively supported
No account manager guidance available until after purchase

buying_signals.signal: Actively building course content during extended trial with week one completed
buying_signals.interpretation: Deeply invested in evaluating Teachable as the platform

buying_signals.signal: Asking very detailed implementation questions about coaching, bundles, and document management
buying_signals.interpretation: Trying to solve real implementation problems, not just browsing

buying_signals.signal: Preparing presentation for leadership team on how the platform will work
buying_signals.interpretation: Championing Teachable internally, building a business case

marketing_data.timeline: Dependent on leadership team presentation and approval; currently in active trial period

calls.segment_reasoning: Pure Life Ministries is delivering a structured counseling program combining digital curriculum with one-on-one coaching sessions, fitting the Coaches & Consultants segment. While they are a nonprofit/ministry, their core use case is individualized coaching at scale paired with course content.

calls.title: Connect With Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-17
ATTENDEES: mert elmas, Naumman Mazıcıoğlu, Zach McCall
COMPANY: DMEMI Fitness Academy
STAGE: Discovery

---
SUMMARY
DMEMI Fitness Academy is a Turkish fitness certification provider (similar to ACE in the United States) that offers trainer certifications for Pilates, fitness instruction, and related disciplines. They currently have course content (PDFs, videos, podcasts) on their website and are looking to build a custom white-labeled mobile application to deliver that content. This was an initial discovery call to understand their requirements before scoping a custom mobile app build.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Deliver fitness certification courses through a branded mobile app
Audience: Aspiring fitness instructors and personal trainers seeking certification
Business model: Selling certification training programs (Pilates, fitness instructor, etc.)
Content types: PDFs, videos, podcasts, images
Scale expectations: Not discussed

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Not discussed
Authority: Naumman Mazıcıoğlu is the owner/decision-maker; mert elmas handles the operational evaluation
Need: Custom white-label mobile app for course delivery with offline access and student profiles
Timeline: Not discussed

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Not discussed
Reporting needs: Not discussed
Payments / checkout: Not discussed
Admin or seat management: Admin managed via web, student experience on mobile app
Special workflows or constraints: White-label requirement (no Teachable branding), offline content access, student profile visibility in the mobile app

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Came to the call with a pre-prepared list of requirements (email from Naumman)
- Owner (Naumman) attended the call personally
- Asked detailed questions about content limits and app capabilities
- Requested to see the mobile app student experience

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Primary interest is the mobile app only, not the full Teachable platform
- Connectivity/audio issues during the call limited depth of conversation
- Need to evaluate Teachable's standard mobile app before deciding on custom scope
- Language barrier may slow communication

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Prospect could not see student profile/course information after downloading the Teachable app (likely not enrolled in anything yet)
- Wanted a demo or sample of the mobile app student experience

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Answer all questions from Naumman's requirements email
- Send recap email
- Send link to download the Teachable mobile app
- Enroll them in a free course so they can see the student-side app experience
- Once custom mobile app requirements are received, scope the build with Tom (engineer)

Customer:
- Download and explore the Teachable mobile app
- Review the standard app experience and identify what additional customizations are needed
- Send detailed custom mobile app requirements to Zach

Scheduled:
- No follow-up meeting scheduled; next interaction via email

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
The call had technical difficulties (audio breaking up, dropped connection). mert elmas is the hands-on evaluator while Naumman Mazıcıoğlu (owner) joined later. A third person was expected but did not join. The conversation was relatively short and high-level due to connectivity issues. They compare themselves to ACE (American Council on Exercise) in the US market. The primary deliverable is scoping a white-label mobile app build.

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KHC4HB9PKYWVJVVYX5QG4VAN

calls.title: Teachable <>  SSFP Followup
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-16
ATTENDEES: Six String Fingerpicking Support, Zach McCall (Teachable)
COMPANY: Six String Fingerpicking
STAGE: Followup

---
SUMMARY
Followup call with Six String Fingerpicking (SSFP), a music education company that teaches fingerpicking guitar. The prospect has been exploring the Teachable sandbox and found it intuitive and similar to their current provider. The main focus of this call was understanding community features, which are critical to their forward model, and clarifying pricing/plan options. They are ready to sign up for the Growth plan.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Migrate existing guitar instruction academy to Teachable, with strong community engagement for students at different skill levels (beginner, intermediate)
Audience: Guitar students globally, various skill levels
Business model: Paid courses with community access as a key retention and engagement tool
Content types: Guitar instruction courses (beginner through intermediate), community forums
Scale expectations: Existing customer base already built out, planning to grow significantly this year

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: PayPal (currently unavailable on their existing platform — high priority)
Payments / checkout: USD-only acceptable (multi-currency nice-to-have but not blocking); PayPal support eliminates international payment friction
Admin or seat management: Need ability to gate community access to paid students only; prevent bot/free sign-ups from accessing community
Special workflows or constraints: Need retroactive community access for migrated students; cross-course community visibility so advanced students can mentor beginners

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- "I think on the whole we're happy with it and we think we're going to join up for the paid version and have a proper go at copying a course over"
- "I think we're ready to jump into the growth one as a minimum. With the number of products we've got and our plans for this year."
- "We take the switch from where we are now onto this and then we're really in it for the long haul and pushing and growing"

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Community feature was unavailable in sandbox — needed live demo to evaluate
- Concern about bot sign-ups and ungated registration (experienced this on current platform)
- Initially confused about self-serve vs enterprise pricing (Jerome had discussed Grace plan previously)

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Platform feels intuitive and similar to current provider
- Community categories/forums structure works for their use case
- Would like cross-course community visibility for peer mentoring

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Growth plan (self-serve) — prospect chose this over the $6,000/yr enterprise plan
Discounts offered: Enterprise pricing mentioned at $6,000/yr with $5,500/yr for 2+ year commitment
Contract length discussed: Prospect opting for self-serve Growth plan to start, may upgrade later

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Be available if prospect wants to upgrade to enterprise plan later

Customer:
- Sign up for Growth plan (self-serve)
- Begin migrating courses and setting up community

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
Prospect is UK-based (references GBP, foreign currency fees). Previously spoke with Jerome who discussed the Grace plan. They have a well-established student base and are switching platforms for the long haul. Community is central to their growth strategy — they want students at different levels to interact and support each other. Very positive and ready to move forward.

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KH1GC7TAXXCNX1Z4BDJWE55R

calls.title: Kesh R and Kevin Codde
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-19
ATTENDEES: Kesh (kesh@keshart.in), Kevin Codde (kevin.codde@teachable.com)
COMPANY: KeshArt
STAGE: Negotiation

---
SUMMARY
KeshArt is an India-based education platform that has exceeded their student limit (approaching 150,000). Kevin presented two options: (1) increase monthly to $375 with unlimited students, or (2) stay at $189/mo and switch to Teachable Pay. Kesh is interested in option 2 but has concerns about Razorpay support since some Indian bank cards fail on Stripe.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Online courses (appears to be art education)
Audience: Indian market primarily
Business model: Free courses with some paid offerings via Stripe/Razorpay
Scale expectations: 150,000+ students

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Currently $189/mo, prefers to stay at that rate
Authority: Kesh appears to be decision-maker
Need: Must resolve student limit overage without price increase
Timeline: Contract by end of February, transition April 1st

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Stripe (current), Razorpay (for Indian cards that fail on Stripe), WordPress (current site)
Payments / checkout: Needs Razorpay support for Indian bank cards; currently uses Zapier to bridge Stripe
Special workflows or constraints: Some Indian bank cards cannot process through Stripe, requiring Razorpay as fallback

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Kesh engaged positively with option 2 (Teachable Pay at $189)
- Asked detailed questions about transition process
- Agreed to April 1st timeline

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Razorpay integration uncertainty. If Teachable Pay cannot handle Indian bank cards, the deal may not work.
- Concern about long-term contract commitment
- Need to verify existing Stripe subscribers can migrate

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Current $189/mo vs $375/mo option
Discounts offered: Stay at $189 with Teachable Pay switch, 2.5% transaction fee, 0% back office fee
Contract length discussed: No long-term commitment, just locked into 2.5% rate
Constraints or approvals needed: Need to verify Razorpay/Indian card support

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:
- Send contract with both options by email today
- Investigate Razorpay/Indian card compatibility with Teachable Pay
- Include April 1st start date

Customer:
- Send examples of failed card payments to Kevin
- Review and sign contract by end of February

Scheduled:
- Transition target: April 1st

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KHVG2TY3NAGV2ATCNM5KB3YP

calls.title: Natasha Perry and Kevin Codde
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-19
ATTENDEES: Natasha "Tasha" Perry (perry.natasha04@gmail.com), Kevin Codde (kevin.codde@teachable.com)
COMPANY: Perry Lifestyle Support
STAGE: Discovery / Demo

---
SUMMARY
Tasha is a nurse by background building a premium, non-clinical lifestyle support service for older adults in Hampshire, UK. She needs a training hub for employee onboarding (7 core courses + 11 add-ons) and an external-facing website for families and referrers. Currently exploring Teachable vs Thinkific.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Employee training hub for lifestyle support workers + external website for client/referral inquiries
Audience: Internal staff (5-10 initially, scaling later) + external families/referrers (GPS, pharmacists, social prescribers)
Business model: Self-funded premium service for affluent areas; free core training for staff, paid add-on skills later
Content types: Self-paced training modules, voiceover videos, competency assessments, policies/procedures, certificates
Scale expectations: 5-10 employees initially, growth expected

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Core plan at $416/month discussed, no pushback on price
Authority: Tasha is sole decision-maker
Need: Staff onboarding and training tracking, external professional presence for credibility
Timeline: Decision in next couple of weeks, absolute latest April

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Reporting needs: Completion tracking, quiz scores, progress reports per user
Admin or seat management: Different access levels for different user types
Special workflows or constraints: DBS check document upload needed (not currently supported), onboarding checklist tracking

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- "That core plan sounds good"
- Decision timeline of "next couple of weeks"
- No price objection to $416/month
- Responded positively to drip content and completion tracking demos

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Also evaluating Thinkific
- Wants design support Teachable doesn't offer
- Self-described poor design skills may slow implementation
- Document upload feature gap (DBS checks)

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Core plan at $5,000/year ($416/month)
Discounts offered: None discussed
Contract length discussed: Annual

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:

calls.title: Connect With Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-19
ATTENDEES: Celine Bencivinni (celine.bencivinni@axio-formation.com), Leeroy Woussen (Axio-Formation), Zach McCall (zach.mccall@teachable.com)
COMPANY: Axio-Formation
STAGE: Discovery / Demo

---
SUMMARY
Axio-Formation is a French professional training company funded by the French government. They deliver short (3-day) e-learning programs for professional skills (AI, Word, Excel, etc.). Currently using Digiforma which has limitations, particularly around file upload feedback workflows. Evaluating Teachable as a replacement LMS.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: LMS for government-funded short professional development courses
Audience: French professionals, students find them via government platform or company website
Business model: Government-funded, courses are free for students
Content types: Self-paced e-learning, live coaching, videos with French audio, diagnostic assessments, file upload exercises
Scale expectations: Not specified, need up to 50,000 students total

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: $6,500/year discussed ($6,000 with multi-year), plus $3,000-5,000 for AI tutor setup
Authority: Leeroy translating for team, Celine also on call
Need: Replace Digiforma, need better file upload workflow, adaptive learning, gamification
Timeline: Follow-up scheduled for Monday

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Need French language support for platform UI and content
Reporting needs: Strict government compliance tracking of student progress
Special workflows or constraints: Diagnostic test at course start to place students at appropriate level (via API), file upload exercises with optional auto-pass, 90% video completion requirement (asked to customize threshold)

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Follow-up meeting already scheduled for Monday
- Asked for test platform access
- Detailed questions about specific workflows

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Adaptive learning requires API work, not built-in
- File upload feedback is not automated (Digiforma does this)
- Video completion threshold is fixed at 90% (prospect wanted flexibility)
- AI tutor is an additional $3,000-5,000
- Language barrier on calls

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: $6,500/year ($6,000 with multi-year) for up to 50,000 students
Discounts offered: $500/year for multi-year commitment
Contract length discussed: Multi-year option mentioned

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Send recap email with plan details
- Send Teachable U access for student experience preview
- Meet again Monday at 5pm CET

Customer:
- Explore Teachable U as student

calls.title: Connect With Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-18
ATTENDEES: Charlie Durkin (contact@coxcoach.com), Zach McCall (zach.mccall@teachable.com)
COMPANY: CoxCoach
STAGE: Discovery / Demo

---
SUMMARY
Charlie Durkin from CoxCoach is evaluating LMS platforms with student experience as the top priority. Their current LMS is visually unappealing and not engaging. He's casting a wide net across multiple platforms. Also runs a website on Framer and wants course content to integrate with it. Has B2B clients needing group management and reporting.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Better student/learner experience with engaging UI, gamification, and smooth course navigation
Audience: Both B2C and B2B clients
Business model: Coaching business with course content, B2B group management
Content types: Video, PDFs, quizzes

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: $6,500/year pricing discussed, no pushback
Authority: Charlie appears to be decision-maker
Need: Better learner UX than current LMS, B2B features, embeddable content
Timeline: Early stage evaluation, "go off and think about it"

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- "It's definitely better than what we've currently got"
- "Worth a consideration on our end"
- Asked about B2B features proactively

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Early evaluation stage, casting wide net
- "I'll probably just go off and think about it"
- Student experience is the make-or-break criterion

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: $6,500/year

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Send recap email with plan details

Customer:
- Evaluate and compare with other platforms
- Book another call if interested

---

calls.title: Devynn Kiesman and Kevin Codde
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-18
ATTENDEES: Devynn Kiesman (dkiesman@herd.com), Kevin Codde (kevin.codde@teachable.com)
COMPANY: Herd
STAGE: Discovery / Demo

---
SUMMARY
Herd is a trucking accessories company with ~2,000 active dealers. Their training director currently trains dealers manually via virtual sessions. They need an organized training platform for 10-15 courses covering product lines, installation, and setup. Training is free for dealers, with a $100/person marketing fund incentive for completion.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Organized dealer training platform replacing manual virtual training sessions
Audience: ~1,500 active dealers with parts people, installation people, and sales people
Business model: Free training for dealers, incentivized with marketing fund dollars ($100/person)
Content types: Slide decks, installation videos, voiceovers, quizzes, certificates
Scale expectations: 1,500 dealers, 10-15 courses initially

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Not discussed explicitly
Authority: Devynn working with training director, has exec push to get started ASAP
Need: Replace manual training process that's hard to track and scale
Timeline: ASAP, exec pressure to launch quickly

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: SSO/login sync with existing dealer website, Microsoft Teams for webinars
Reporting needs: Per-organization reporting, per-tag filtering (parts/installation/sales), completion tracking for incentive programs
Admin or seat management: Bulk user upload, organization-level seat management, user tagging by department
Special workflows or constraints: Course completion gating (finish one to unlock next), branded certificates, white-label emails

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- "Sounds like we're getting a push from the execs to get this going ASAP"
- "I think we'll want to get started pretty soon"
- "That sounds like good next step" (re: contract)
- Certificates are a definite want

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- SSO integration needs API work, unknown cost and feasibility
- Course-to-course completion gating may not be supported (Kevin checking)
- Not sure how dealers will respond to the system

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:
- Send contract for review
- Answer SSO/login sync question
- Check course-to-course completion gating
- Send account setup link

Customer:
- Review contract
- Work with training director on content preparation (voiceovers)
- Start building courses once account is set up

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KHRV5JJXE3WMYGXV6RGBZ7YD

calls.title: Rodrigo Monje and Kevin Codde
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-18
ATTENDEES: Rodrigo Monje (rmonje.av@gmail.com), Kevin Codde (kevin.codde@teachable.com)
COMPANY: Media Pro (Adobe Training Center)
STAGE: Negotiation

---
SUMMARY
Rodrigo runs Media Pro, the first Adobe Training Center in Latin America (since 2005). They have 4 Teachable schools: AdobeDuca (free teacher courses), Adobe Tech Academy (PDF/Acrobat courses, underperforming), Media Pro Online Training Center (paid certification courses), and a fourth. Flagged for exceeding student limits. Kevin proposed two options: partnership/case study for unlimited access, or customized enterprise plan (~$30K/year for all 4 accounts).

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Scaled online design education complementing in-person classes, plus Adobe certification prep courses
Audience: Teachers in Latin America (free), professionals seeking Adobe certification (paid)
Business model: Free courses for teachers (Adobe-partnered), paid certification courses, transitioning to membership model ($3/month all-access)
Content types: Design courses, Adobe certification prep, on-demand webinars (planned)
Scale expectations: 100K+ registered users across schools (many inactive), 35-40 paying members currently

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: ~$30K/year for all 4 accounts discussed
Authority: Rodrigo is partner, needs to discuss with co-partner and Adobe marketing team
Need: User cleanup, account restructuring, unlimited student capacity
Timeline: Follow-up March 9th, team on vacation for a few weeks

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Open to case study partnership ("sounds good")
- Plans to expand to paid webinars by July/August
- Actively cleaning up data to get accurate usage

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Needs internal discussion with partner and Adobe marketing team
- Team on vacation, decisions delayed
- Might clean up users and reduce below limits (avoiding need for upgrade)

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Customized plan for all 4 accounts at ~$30,000/year (~$2,712/account/year, ~$226/month/account)
Discounts offered: Partnership option (case study/reference) for unlimited users at current pricing
Constraints or approvals needed: Adobe marketing team approval, co-partner agreement

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:
- Send email with both options (customized plan and partnership proposal)
- Schedule follow-up for March 9th at 1pm Chile time

Customer:
- Discuss with partner and Adobe marketing team
- Clean up inactive users across all portals
- Decide on partnership vs. paid plan

Scheduled:
- March 9th follow-up call

---

calls.title: Tom Darling and Kevin Codde
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-24
ATTENDEES: Tom Darling, Will Wheeler
COMPANY: City of Albuquerque
STAGE: Negotiation

---
SUMMARY
City of Albuquerque Employee Learning Center, existing Teachable customer since ~2016. They use Teachable alongside Knowledge City ($60K/yr LMS) and PeopleSoft ELM for government employee training. Currently over plan limits on courses (51 published, need ~75) and users (16K+). Negotiating a custom enterprise plan that stays under $2,500/yr P-card limit.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Deliver mandatory and optional training to city employees, seasonal workers, volunteers, boards/commissions, and public
Audience: ~3,000 active learners/year, 500-1000 seasonal summer hires, boards/commissions, public
Business model: Internal government training (all free courses, no billing)
Content types: Custom courses, compliance training, safety certifications
Scale expectations: ~75 published courses, up to 20K user accounts

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Must stay under $2,500/yr (P-card limit including tax). Current Knowledge City license is $60K/yr for comparison.
Authority: Tom Darling (Associate Director of HR) has budget authority up to $2,500 P-card. Multi-year agreements require legal review (~1 month).
Need: Strong. Currently over plan limits, need more courses for Spanish content and Environmental Health dept.
Timeline: Immediate. Account already adjusted to Advanced plan, need resolution.

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: ClassMarker for testing (considering replacing with Teachable quizzes)
Reporting needs: Completion data, certificate tracking, inactive user reporting, leaderboard
Payments / checkout: Purchase order / invoice billing (government requirement), tax exemption
Admin or seat management: Need more than 5 admin seats, want limited/role-based admin access
Special workflows: Certificate auto-delivery to supervisors, Spanish course duplication

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Been with Teachable 10+ years, very loyal
- Willing to be a customer reference
- Would like multi-year agreement if discount is significant enough
- Love the product, recommended to other cities

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- $2,500 P-card limit is hard ceiling including tax
- Multi-year agreements require legal review (~1 month)
- City is in budget crunch
- Can't use ACH for recurring payments, only one-time

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Quiz/testing capabilities have improved enough to potentially replace ClassMarker
- Reporting has improved significantly (leaderboard)
- Multi-language support still requires separate courses
- Need better inactive user data/management

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Custom enterprise plan (~75 courses, ~20K users)
Discounts offered: Kevin to present proposal at ~$2,400/yr
Contract length discussed: Multi-year with discount, but legal review required
Constraints: Must stay under $2,500/yr including tax (P-card limit)

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:
- Present proposal to internal team
- Send proposal by end of day
- Check on inactive user data capabilities

Customer:
- Will and Tom to research actual active user counts
- Review proposal when received

Scheduled:
- Kevin to follow up same day with proposal

calls.title: Meeting with Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-24
ATTENDEES: Jeremiah Temoney (temoney83@gmail.com)
COMPANY: Temoney Company (name not captured in transcript)
STAGE: Demo

---
SUMMARY
First discovery + demo call with Jeremiah Temoney. His company is looking to move all in-person staff training online. They run compliance trainings (defensive driving, CPR) for their employees and want onboarding for new hires plus recurring annual trainings. Looking to launch by June or July. Approximately 20 courses needed.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Move in-person staff training to an online platform
Audience: Company employees (new hires + all staff)
Business model: Internal employee training (all free courses)
Content types: Compliance training (defensive driving, CPR), onboarding, videos/text/images
Scale expectations: ~20 courses, unknown number of employees

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Not discussed explicitly. Core plan at $5,000/yr was presented.
Authority: Jeremiah is evaluating but needs to bring info back to his team.
Need: Strong. Currently doing all training in-person and want to move online.
Timeline: June or July target launch.

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Not discussed
Reporting needs: Quiz scores, completion tracking, CSV export
Payments / checkout: N/A (internal training, all free courses)
Admin or seat management: Multiple departments, organizations feature, up to 15 admins on Core plan
Special workflows: Certifications at end of courses, department-based grouping

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Scheduled a follow-up meeting for Friday at 12:15 PM ET
- Plans to bring the rest of the team to the next call
- Engaged positively throughout the demo

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Needs team buy-in before moving forward
- Company name not captured (Fireflies only got one side of audio)

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Core package
Price: $5,000/yr (monthly billing available)
Capacity: 15 admins, 2,000 students, 25 published courses
Perks: 7-day free trial, custom branding, organizations, SSO, integrations, analytics

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:
- Send follow-up email with pricing overview and one-pagers
- Send calendar invite for Friday 12:15 PM ET follow-up

Customer:
- Share materials with team
- Bring team to Friday follow-up call

Scheduled:
- Next meeting: Friday at 12:15 PM ET (30 min)
- Materials to send: Pricing one-pagers, platform overview

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KJ84E0HMJZ19VTNFGS06S3DG
