calls.title: Mila Oliveira and Kevin Codde
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-23
ATTENDEES: Mila Oliveira
COMPANY: Motors 1
STAGE: Demo

---
SUMMARY
Motors 1 is a transport, traffic, and parking (TTP) planning company headquartered in Madrid with global operations, especially strong in the Middle East. They previously started a Teachable project ~2 years ago but paused because the platform "wasn't very intuitive for those creating the content" and had formatting issues. Mila Oliveira, who recently joined Motors 1 from a major events background (Olympics, World Cup), is restarting the initiative. They want to standardize internal training first, then sell courses externally.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Restart internal training platform for standardized knowledge delivery, then expand to selling courses externally to capitalize on their 15+ years of TTP expertise
Audience: ~300-400 internal employees initially, scaling with seasonal peaks (e.g., 300+ traffic marshals for Saudi Arabia projects); external clients in a second wave
Business model: Free internal training for employees; paid courses for external audiences
Content types: Mix of pictures from past projects, operational videos, multi-format resources across 90 different topics (~900 modules ready)
Scale expectations: 400 starting users with seasonal peaks, 90+ individual courses

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Not explicitly discussed; $5,000/year commitment presented by Kevin (Core plan, monthly billing)
Authority: Mila is evaluating and presenting to senior management; decision rests with upper management
Need: Strong. Company growing exponentially, restructuring from temporary hires to fixed project managers, need standardized training at scale
Timeline: ASAP per management. Middle East summer season starts late September, training needs to begin end of August. Mila has management presentation early next week.

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Website link embed (had this before)
Reporting needs: Not explicitly discussed
Payments / checkout: Need free access for internal users and paid access for external users on same courses
Admin or seat management: 15 admin users sufficient; content creation focused on project managers
Special workflows: Content protection / IP safeguarding (proprietary TTP expertise), GDPR compliance, SOC 2 Type 2 security, easy course editing/updates post-publish, mentoring/coaching sessions

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- "It looks like what we are looking for" — positive demo reaction
- "I see no reason to don't continue with you guys" — previous relationship loyalty
- Management has mandated this be done "as soon as possible"
- Mila presenting offers to management early next week
- Willing to share something for next week
- Positive reaction to platform intuitiveness (previous concern was resolved)

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Previous bad experience with content creation intuitiveness and formatting bugs (~2 years ago)
- Evaluating other platforms (mentioned having "other calls to attend")
- Decision requires senior management approval
- Mila is new to company and relying on predecessor's feedback about past Teachable issues

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Previous concern about intuitiveness appears resolved: "So far it looks very intuitive for me. It doesn't seem like a monster."
- Previous formatting bugs were a dealbreaker (content appearing differently after platform restart)
- Positive reception of organizations feature for department segmentation
- Community feature got positive reaction

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Core plan with 100 published courses (normally Pro feature), 2000 students, 15 admins, organizations, SSO, dedicated CSM
Discounts offered: Pro-level course limit (100) on Core pricing
Contract length discussed: Annual commitment ($5,000/year, monthly billing)
Constraints: Must present competitive offers to senior management alongside other vendor proposals

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:
- Send written proposal with pricing and one-pagers on platform functionality
- Be available for follow-up questions

Customer:
- Mila to present compiled offers to senior management early next week
- Will share decision/feedback within one week

Scheduled:
- No specific follow-up meeting set; Mila to reach out after management review

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
Mila is enthusiastic and engaged but clearly evaluating multiple vendors. She has a major events background (Olympics, World Cup) which may mean high standards for operations. The company is global with offices in Madrid, Oxford, and Germany. The Middle East (especially Saudi Arabia) is a key market with seasonal peaks driving training demand. Previous Teachable champion left the company; Mila is working from secondhand feedback about past issues.

calls.title: Connect With Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-20
ATTENDEES: Kyle (kyle@refinerypass.com), Zach McCall (zach.mccall@teachable.com), Jerome Olaloye (jerome.olaloye@teachable.com)
COMPANY: RefineryPass
STAGE: Demo

---
SUMMARY
Kyle from RefineryPass, a safety training company for refinery contractors, is looking to migrate off Docebo due to cost. They contracted for 2,000 users/month but are down to ~800, and Docebo won't renegotiate. He's evaluating Teachable and Thinkific, but expressed strong preference for Teachable based on pricing, design flexibility, and simplicity. Kyle is the creative director and technical lead; his business partner Rick was not on the call.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Deliver and sell safety training courses to refinery contractors (B2B)
Audience: Field workers/contractors at refineries across multiple US states (Ohio, Utah, North Dakota)
Business model: B2B — refineries contract with RefineryPass to provide safety training to their contractors; also has a catalog of safety content
Content types: Video-based safety courses with legal agreements, quizzes/tests, and compliance tracking
Scale expectations: ~800 current monthly users, potentially growing to ~1,000; 6 B2B clients currently; recently partnered with Ammonia Institute (ASTI)

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Currently paying significantly more with Docebo; $6,500/yr Teachable pricing described as "extremely reasonable"
Authority: Kyle is operational lead but has a business partner (Rick) who needs to review; Kyle's wife is also a web developer involved in the business
Need: Cost-effective LMS with course selling, compliance tracking, admin visibility per client/location, and modern design
Timeline: Docebo contract ends July 2027; actively trying to exit early ("pull the plug early"). Wants Teachable "in my back pocket" to switch quickly

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Shopify SSO (current front-end), Wix (sister company), potential Teachable site builder migration
Reporting needs: Per-location/client admin reporting — teachers must verify student course completion and compliance
Payments / checkout: Course transactions (one-off purchases); B2B contracts done off-platform then seats granted
Admin or seat management: Organizations feature for per-refinery grouping; admin/teacher roles to check student compliance
Special workflows or constraints: Legal agreement acceptance before course content; video + test sequence; SCORM nice-to-have but not required (Q2/Q3 roadmap noted)

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- "I don't see any flies in the ointment. To be honest, I don't feel like I would be going anywhere else at this point."
- "I love the price. I love the functionality."
- "I needed something in my back pocket where I could just be like Jerome, Zach, let's go."
- Asked about annual billing structure — pricing engagement
- Requested example site links to share with business partner Rick
- Expressed wanting to exit Docebo contract early

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Business partner Rick not on the call — needs to review before decision
- Financial ties to Shopify that won't end until later this year
- SCORM not yet available (Q2/Q3) — currently using SCORM on Docebo but says he can work without it
- Needs to coordinate early exit from Docebo contract

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Very positive on design flexibility — surprised at variety of Teachable sites
- Appreciates Organizations feature for per-location management
- Interested in AI chatbot/support bot capability
- Values that custom dev work is available and subsidized for new customers
- Docebo's front-end "looked like ass... straight out of 1999"
- Docebo's feature request process (blog queue voting) was frustrating

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: $6,500/year; $6,000/year for 2+ year commitment
Discounts offered: $500/year multi-year discount; subsidized custom dev work at signup
Contract length discussed: Annual billing; multi-year option mentioned
Constraints or approvals needed: Business partner Rick must review; Docebo contract exit timing

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Send recap email with pricing
- Send menu of custom dev services/add-ons
- Send links to example Teachable sites shown during demo
- Send call recording for Rick to review

Customer:
- Share recording and materials with business partner Rick
- Determine Docebo contract exit timeline
- Follow up when ready to move forward

Scheduled:
- Next meeting date: TBD pending Rick's review
- Materials to send: Pricing recap, services menu, example site links, recording

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
Kyle is casual, direct, and technically competent — he builds sites himself on Wix and is the creative director. Very pragmatic about needs ("I need three things to make money"). Has a sister company that also uses an LMS. Recently partnered with ASTI (Ammonia Institute) for ammonia safety training, indicating growth potential. Strong personality, fast-moving, values simplicity and modern design over feature bloat. Gut feel: high intent, likely to convert once Rick signs off and Docebo exit is sorted.

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KHS96BMM38NAQS36PEFJB6T3

calls.segment_reasoning: RefineryPass delivers mandatory safety training for refinery contractors — compliance-driven, certification-tracked courses that field workers must complete before entering job sites. The need for completion tracking, legal agreements, and admin verification of student compliance are hallmarks of the Continuing Education & Credentialing segment.

competitor_mentions.context: RefineryPass is currently on Docebo but looking to leave. They contracted for 2,000 users/month but usage dropped to ~800 after losing clients, and Docebo won't renegotiate pricing. Kyle described Docebo as more of an internal compliance tool that doesn't fit their B2B course-selling model. He criticized the front-end design as looking 'straight out of 1999' and the feature request process as a frustrating public vote queue.

Kyle mentioned Thinkific as the other platform he's looking at alongside Teachable. He said he's been 'poking around' but hasn't pursued them hard, and noted he's 'drawn towards Teachable' for reasons he can't fully explain. Later asked for the Teachable pitch against Thinkific.

calls.title: Airielle Taylor and Kevin Codde
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-20
ATTENDEES: Airielle Taylor (airielle.taylor@gmail.com)
COMPANY: Train the Trainers (Basketball)
STAGE: Demo

---
SUMMARY
Airielle Taylor is building a "Train the Trainers" basketball coaching program that is expanding internationally, starting with Libya. They currently train in person with printed materials but have secured investors to scale to an online platform. She is evaluating platforms and wants to select one by mid-March to early April, with a September launch target for 1,500 coaches in year one, scaling to 30,000 in three years.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Move an in-person basketball coaching curriculum to an online platform for international trainer certification
Audience: Basketball coaches, starting with Libya, expanding to other countries
Business model: Paid courses with bulk/group enrollment options; investors funding the expansion
Content types: 8-10 video-heavy courses, quizzes, coaching sessions, digital downloads
Scale expectations: 1,500 coaches year 1, 30,000 participants in 3 years

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: $5,000/year core package fits "perfectly" within their budget
Authority: Airielle appears to be the primary decision-maker (also a professor, creates curriculum)
Need: Must move from in-person printed materials to scalable online platform for international expansion
Timeline: Platform selected by mid-March to early April; content built March-April; platform build May-June; trainer training August; coach training September

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Not discussed
Reporting needs: Lesson completion tracking, quiz scores, leaderboards, CSV export
Payments / checkout: One-time purchase, subscriptions, and bulk/group payments for organizations enrolling multiple coaches
Admin or seat management: Up to 15 admin users, single course author (Airielle)
Special workflows or constraints: Courses needed in English, Arabic, and French (multi-language requirement)

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Budget fits "perfectly" — no pushback on $5,000/year pricing
- Knows others who use the platform: "I know others who use your platform as well"
- Wants Teachable to build the courses (white glove service) — strong commitment signal
- Expressed readiness: "Everything looks great to me"
- Clear, aggressive timeline with investor backing

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Platform stability concern: asked what happens if Teachable goes out of business
- Multi-language requirement (English, Arabic, French) may require self-build rather than white glove service
- Getting "so many quotes" — actively evaluating multiple platforms

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Familiar with LMS platforms (uses Blackboard as a professor) — found Teachable intuitive
- Positive reaction to organizations, reporting, and bundling features

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Core package at $5,000/year
Discounts offered: None
Contract length discussed: Annual, payable monthly
Constraints or approvals needed: White glove course build pricing TBD separately

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:
- Send pricing and plan details in writing
- Get quote for white glove course build service (8 courses)

Customer:
- Select platform by mid-March to early April
- Finalize curriculum with curriculum developer by March-April
- Begin platform build May-June

Scheduled:
- Next meeting date: Not explicitly scheduled
- Materials to send: Written pricing/plan details

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
Airielle was feeling unwell on the call (left camera off, losing her voice) but wanted to proceed rather than reschedule. She is also a professor who works in Blackboard, so she is technically literate with LMS platforms. The organization has investor backing and is clearly serious about the timeline. She is shopping multiple vendors ("getting so many quotes"). The international expansion angle (Libya, multilingual) adds complexity.

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KHXS1RVZETJCZJT6532T5076

calls.title: Lines for Life
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-19
ATTENDEES: Crystal Larson, Annie, Thomas Magee
COMPANY: Lines for Life
STAGE: Demo

---
SUMMARY
Lines for Life is a Portland, Oregon-based nonprofit focused on mental health and crisis intervention. They have 200-400 employees and are evaluating LMS platforms to replace their current low-tech approach (voiced-over PowerPoints, recorded webinars). They need both internal employee training (onboarding, continued education, compliance) and external paid training courses for community organizations.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Replace low-tech asynchronous training (PowerPoints, recorded webinars) with a proper LMS for both internal and external training
Audience: 200-400 internal employees (cohorts of 10-30), plus external community organizations
Business model: Internal training (free), external training (paid per-course, bundles, and memberships)
Content types: Voiced-over presentations, multimedia courses with video/quizzes, live coaching sessions converted to on-demand
Scale expectations: 20-50 courses, up to 2,000 users, 10+ admin users

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: $5,000/yr annual contract discussed; interested in multi-year (1, 3, or 5 year) discounts
Authority: Crystal Larson appears to be coordinating the evaluation; decision involves a broader core team
Need: Strong. Currently using low-tech methods with no completion tracking or engagement measurement
Timeline: Decision by second week of March; trial period next; rollout June/July

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: None discussed explicitly; currently using Zoom for recording
Reporting needs: Completion tracking, automated Excel export, training completion dashboard per user, demographic reporting for funders
Payments / checkout: Per-course purchase, bundles, tiered memberships, group/bulk license purchasing for organizations
Admin or seat management: 10+ admin users, organizations feature for internal/external segmentation
Special workflows: Auto-certificates on completion, pre/post surveys, custom registration fields for demographic collection, auto-tagging users by registration source

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Thomas Magee extremely enthusiastic about mandatory video completion ("That's so beautiful. I want to cry a little.")
- Crystal Larson interested in multi-year contracts ("if we did like one year and then folks were like yeah this is what we're going to do for long term")
- Team asking detailed questions about features — shows serious evaluation
- Annie proactively exploring lead capture and branding customization

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Evaluating multiple LMS platforms simultaneously ("I've talked to so many people about LMS the last few weeks")
- Decision depends on broader team review and scheduling of all demos
- Extended trial requested (7 days may not be enough for their team's schedules)
- Custom registration fields limited to first/last/email/phone; workaround of quiz redirect is an extra step

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Custom form fields on registration are limited (only first, last, email, phone) — prospect needs demographic data for funder reporting
- No time-on-page minimum for text content (Thomas wanted to enforce minimum read time)
- Complete and Continue button text may confuse users (Thomas Magee flagged UX concern)

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Core internal training package at $5,000/yr
Includes: 15 admins, 2,000 students, 25 published courses, custom branding, organizations, SSO, support/onboarding, analytics
Contract length discussed: 1, 3, or 5 year options with multi-year discounts
Payment: Can pay monthly

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:
- Send recording of the demo
- Put pricing/details in writing
- Touch base before mid-March (going on paternity leave)

Customer:
- Bring demo back to core team for review
- Identify top 2-3 LMS platforms for trial by second week of March
- Request extended trial period (14 days preferred)

Scheduled:
- Kevin to follow up before second week of March

calls.title: Koltin <> Teachable | Demo
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-19
ATTENDEES: Alejandro Arriaga, Alejandra Bortoni (Head of Experience), Jorge (Coordinator of Community Operations)
COMPANY: Koltin
STAGE: Demo

---
SUMMARY
Demo call with Koltin, a membership-based organization that serves an elderly community. They are evaluating Teachable as an LMS to deliver live webinars, recorded courses, quizzes, and certifications to their members. Key concern is ease of use for seniors who struggle with email-based account creation. Three stakeholders attended: Alejandro (lead contact), Alejandra (Head of Experience), and Jorge (Coordinator of Community Operations).

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Deliver online courses, live webinars, and recorded content to their membership community
Audience: Elderly members/seniors with limited technology proficiency
Business model: Membership-based organization providing educational content
Content types: Live webinars, recorded video courses, quizzes, certificates
Scale expectations: Considering 5,000-10,000 students; multiple authors (up to 10)

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Discussed both tiers — $10,000/yr (Pro) and $5,000/yr (Core)
Authority: Alejandro appears to be the decision-maker; Alejandra (Head of Experience) and Jorge (Community Ops) are evaluators
Need: Strong — currently have an app and website but need an LMS for course delivery
Timeline: Alejandro said he would give feedback "in a few hours or tomorrow"

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Need to embed/redirect from their existing mobile app to Teachable
Reporting needs: Organization-level reporting, leaderboards, completion tracking
Admin or seat management: Need multiple admin users (10+ authors), organizations to group students
Special workflows or constraints: SSO critical for elderly users who struggle with email login; asked about phone number or client ID login as alternative

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Asked about next steps and pricing proactively
- Requested Kevin send plan comparison to decide between Core and Pro
- Said would give feedback same day or next day
- Three stakeholders attended the demo

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Elderly user base has difficulty with email-based account creation
- May want phone number or member ID login (not natively supported)
- Price sensitivity — asked about lower tier to start with fewer students

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Strong need for alternative login methods beyond email (phone, member ID)
- Need intuitive, simple UX for senior users
- Want to embed Teachable within their existing mobile app

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Pro ($10,000/yr, ~$833/mo) and Core ($5,000/yr)
Discounts offered: Kevin offered to add Organizations feature to Core plan as exception
Contract length discussed: Annual billing
Constraints or approvals needed: Team needs to discuss internally before deciding

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:
- Send plan comparison (Core vs Pro) to Alejandro's email
- Check with product team about phone number login option

Customer:
- Internal discussion and feedback within hours or next day
- Decide between Core and Pro plan

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KHVQR4FFXJMBNQQ3N5Y22ZRZ

calls.title: Casey Caston and Zach McCall
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-17
ATTENDEES: Casey Caston
COMPANY: Marriage365
STAGE: Discovery

---
SUMMARY
Casey Caston is the founder of Marriage365, an online relationship education platform with a custom-built mobile app and web platform. They grew from 1,200 subscribers to 10,000 during COVID but have since seen subscription rates decline. They have 80,000 freemium members and over 1,000 pieces of content. Casey is evaluating Teachable both for potentially migrating Marriage365's content delivery and for a separate, larger national relationship skills initiative project.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Migrate content delivery from custom-built platform to reduce maintenance costs; explore white-label app for new national relationship skills initiative
Audience: 80,000 freemium members, declining paid subscriber base (was 10K at peak)
Business model: Freemium with paid subscriptions (exploring shift to one-time purchases due to subscription fatigue)
Content types: Video courses, worksheets, podcast, push notifications
Scale expectations: 80,000+ users, 1,000+ content pieces

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Currently spending six figures/year on dev maintenance (AWS, dev time, RevenueCat for app subscriptions). Teachable base cost quoted at $7,500/year.
Authority: Casey is the founder and decision-maker, but needs ops team involvement for technical scoping.
Need: Strong. Current platform maintenance costs are unsustainable relative to declining subscription revenue.
Timeline: Q2 2026 project. Team is recovering from a book launch. Early conversations to assess feasibility.

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Custom mobile app (white-label needed), existing website embedding
Reporting needs: Not discussed in detail
Payments / checkout: Currently split between Braintree (web) and App Store subscriptions (RevenueCat). Need migration path to Stripe.
Admin or seat management: Not discussed
Special workflows: Content migration for 1,000+ pieces, user migration for 80K accounts (batches of 20K suggested), white-label app build

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Spending $100K+/year on platform maintenance, sees Teachable as potential 90% cost reduction
- Actively exploring with product team review of their app
- Requested follow-up meeting between Teachable product team and his ops team
- Wants Teachable product team to subscribe and evaluate their current app

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Massive migration scope: 80K users, 1K+ content pieces, two payment systems
- Custom-built platform has features that may not map 1:1 to Teachable
- Team bandwidth limited (post-book-launch recovery)
- App Store subscription migration complexity
- Potential loss of some flexibility from custom-built platform

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Interested in white-label app capability
- Attracted to community features (does not have currently)
- Subscription fatigue concern — interested in one-time purchase model
- Wants product team to validate they can replicate current app experience

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Enterprise/custom ($7,500/yr base mentioned)
Discounts offered: None yet
Contract length discussed: Not discussed
Constraints: Cost savings vs current $100K+/yr maintenance is the key driver

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Send call recap/notes to Casey
- Talk to Tom (app builder) about replicating Marriage365 app experience
- Coordinate product team review

Customer:
- Share information with his ops/dev team
- Provide app access for Teachable product team review

Scheduled:
- No specific date set; follow-up after both teams review

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
Casey is a founder/content creator personality — warm, direct, action-oriented. He has a second project (national relationship skills initiative) that could be an even better fit for Teachable since it would be built from scratch. The Marriage365 migration is more complex but the cost savings argument is compelling. He mentioned being in the middle of making a protein shake when the call started — casual, approachable demeanor. This feels like a real opportunity if the migration scoping goes well.

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KH2W1XVD9N67H435T4ECAJ34

marketing_data.company_description: Online relationship education platform offering video courses, worksheets, podcasts, and a mobile app for marriage and relationship skills development. Grew rapidly during COVID from 1,200 to 10,000 subscribers, with 80,000 freemium members.
currently_evaluating: Payment system migration from Braintree/App Store to Stripe

quotes.text: There's a lot of infrastructure that we've invested in over the past three years. It is a ground up build, so we spent a lot of money on it.
quotes.speaker: Casey Caston

quotes.text: We walked in, we probably had 1200 subscribers. Covid happened online learning was fringe. And we jumped to 8,000 by the end of the year, 10,000 within like 1Q21.
quotes.speaker: Casey Caston

quotes.text: I do think that subscription fatigue set in. So we're kind of now post subscription.
quotes.speaker: Casey Caston

quotes.text: If I can make a case for the fact that we are spending $100,000 a year just in dev costs... this could be a game changer for us real quick.
quotes.speaker: Casey Caston

quotes.text: So moving over and training all of those people to me seems almost like a daunting task.
quotes.speaker: Casey Caston

quotes.text: I think I'm more interested in this idea of how teachable can be white labeled for something.
quotes.speaker: Casey Caston

calls.title: Sawan Kapoor and Kevin Codde
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-17
ATTENDEES: Sawan Kapoor (sawankapoor@hotmail.com), Manish (Success Gyan team)
COMPANY: Success Gyan
STAGE: Negotiation

---
SUMMARY
Sawan Kapoor runs the career coaching vertical within Success Gyan, the largest webinar and seminar company in India with 10 verticals and ~150,000 new clients per vertical per year. They have been on Teachable for 5-6 years and were flagged due to exceeding the Growth plan's 5,000-user limit. Sawan was quoted a ~$12,000/yr increase (~600% over current $190/mo) and is pushing back hard, citing a prior Teachable support screenshot promising unlimited students on Growth. He wants to stay but will migrate if forced into a large increase.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Host career coaching courses for students who convert through webinar funnels
Audience: Indian professionals seeking career advancement, coaching certifications
Business model: Paid courses (payment collected via Razorpay locally, enrollment via Zapier integration)
Content types: Video courses across multiple verticals (career coaching, life coaching, neuroscience, spiritual healing, personal branding)
Scale expectations: 25 courses at Growth plan cap, 10 verticals with 150K new clients/year each, planning a second school for personal leadership

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Currently paying ~$190/mo (~$2,280/yr). Previous plan was ~$1,000/yr before price increase. Cannot absorb 600% increase — India is price-sensitive market, cannot pass costs to customers.
Authority: Sawan runs career coaching vertical but cannot make decisions for other verticals (e.g., switching to Teachable Pay requires buy-in from other vertical heads).
Need: Course hosting, reliable video streaming, unlimited (or high-cap) student enrollment, international payment support.
Timeline: Immediate — team already started looking at alternatives after receiving the price increase email.

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Zapier (enrollment automation from local payment gateway to Teachable), Razorpay (Indian payment gateway)
Reporting needs: Not discussed
Payments / checkout: Local Indian payment gateway (Razorpay) due to RBI regulations on international money movement; W2 complications with direct Teachable payments; declined Teachable Pay switch due to complexity and multi-vertical governance
Admin or seat management: Multi-vertical ecosystem, each vertical has its own head; Sawan cannot mandate changes across verticals
Special workflows or constraints: Indian regulatory environment (Reserve Bank of India), international payout compliance issues

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- 5-6 year loyal customer, absorbed prior 2x price increase without complaint
- Actively recommends Teachable to coached coaches (estimated 1,000+ coaches on Teachable because of Success Gyan)
- Planning to launch a second school (personal leadership) in Q1-Q2 of Indian FY (April-Sept 2026)
- Expressed desire to stay: "we want to stay with Teachable... it's a beautiful symbiotic relationship"

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- 600% price increase is a dealbreaker — "I'd be in losses"
- Team already started evaluating alternatives
- Teachable support told them unlimited students on Growth — has screenshot evidence
- Cannot switch to Teachable Pay due to Indian payment regulations and multi-vertical governance
- Video buffering issues at 55-60 min mark degrading student experience
- Customer support quality insufficient — sends articles instead of hands-on help
- If they leave, all downstream Teachable referrals from 10 verticals stop (organic growth channel lost)

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Videos buffering at 55 min to 1 hour mark
- Customer support sends articles instead of providing direct help
- Growth plan 25-course limit is at capacity
- International payout process is too complicated (W2 requirement)

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Currently on Growth (~$190/mo). Previously on highest plan (~$1,000/yr before increase). Kevin suggested ~$7,000/yr — rejected.
Discounts offered: Kevin exploring options, mentioned Teachable Pay switch could avoid increase.
Contract length discussed: Currently annual billing.
Constraints or approvals needed: Multi-vertical governance — Sawan cannot mandate Teachable Pay switch for all verticals.

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:
- Take screenshots (from Sawan showing unlimited students promise) back to team
- Come back with a retention offer today
- Explore what pricing team can work out given the support screenshot error

Customer:
- Send screenshots of Teachable support confirming unlimited students on Growth
- Continue evaluating alternatives in parallel

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
Sawan is articulate, firm but polite. He clearly framed the conversation as a retention issue rather than a negotiation — emphasizing the downstream referral value Success Gyan brings. He was strategic in mentioning the volume of coaches they've driven to Teachable and the organic growth that would cease. Manish (team member) was mostly silent. The call had a warm, professional tone. Kevin was empathetic but limited in what he could offer on the spot.

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KHP2NW7T2ADENB2HJDBMJ9SP

calls.title: Jozeph Danial and Kevin Codde
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-17
ATTENDEES: jozephdanial@gmail.com
COMPANY: Jozeph Danial
STAGE: Negotiation

---
SUMMARY
Jozeph Ezzat Danial is an Egyptian course creator with ~800k social media followers across Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok. He sells low-ticket courses (~$5 each) to a young Egyptian audience, collecting payments via E-wallets (mobile electronic wallets) outside of Teachable Pay. He is on the Growth plan and was surprised by the $12,000/year price increase related to exceeding the 5,000 student limit. Kevin proposed a custom plan at ~$7,000 with unlimited students.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Sell affordable educational courses to young Arabic-speaking audience
Audience: Young Egyptians and MENA region learners, mostly without credit cards
Business model: High-volume, low-ticket ($5/course) with manual E-wallet payment collection
Content types: Courses with assignments, certificates, gamification
Scale expectations: Already exceeding 5,000 students, growing via 800k social following

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Current plan cost is a concern; $12K/year too high, competitor offers pay-per-student at $0.75
Authority: Jozeph is the sole decision-maker
Need: Needs unlimited students, E-wallet payment support, affordable plan pricing
Timeline: Immediate — contract renewal negotiation in progress

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Payments / checkout: E-wallet support (not Stripe/credit card); manual enrollment by email with moderator team handling screenshot-based payment verification
Admin or seat management: Needs unlimited student capacity; currently managing by deleting inactive students
Special workflows or constraints: Payment collection happens entirely outside Teachable via E-wallets; moderator team handles verification manually

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- "That's why I love teachable and I'm still with teachable and I didn't go to any other platform"
- Agreed to follow-up meeting on Thursday
- Open to reviewing custom plan proposal

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Price sensitivity: $12K/year is prohibitive; says he could build his own platform for a fraction
- Competitor leverage: Unnamed competitor offers pay-per-student at $0.75 with no subscription
- Payment method incompatibility: Audience cannot use credit cards; Stripe/Teachable Pay don't support E-wallets
- Student limit frustration: Was not aware of 5,000 student cap when subscribing

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Loves gamification, assignments, and certificates
- Wants E-wallet or mobile payment gateway integration
- Student limit was not clearly communicated during subscription

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Growth plan, currently ~$12K/year due to student overage
Discounts offered: Kevin proposed custom plan at ~$7,000 with unlimited students
Contract length discussed: Annual
Constraints or approvals needed: Jozeph wants to see formal proposal before committing

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:
- Put together custom plan proposal with unlimited students at ~$7,000
- Send proposal via email
- Schedule follow-up meeting on Thursday

Customer:
- Review the custom plan proposal
- Meet again on Thursday to discuss

Scheduled:
- Next meeting date: Thursday (same week)
- Materials to send: Custom plan contract/proposal

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
Jozeph is a passionate educator focused on accessibility, not profit maximization. He has significant social media reach (800k followers) and is converting followers to Teachable students. He genuinely likes the platform but is price-sensitive and has a legitimate payment infrastructure gap (E-wallets vs credit cards in Egypt). The unnamed competitor offering per-student pricing is a real risk.

---

calls.title: Alejandro Arriaga and Kevin Codde
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-12
ATTENDEES: Alejandro Arriaga (alejandro.arriaga@koltin.mx)
COMPANY: Koltin
STAGE: Demo

---
SUMMARY
Alejandro Arriaga from Koltin, a Mexican senior membership organization, is looking for a digital learning platform to extend their in-person community classes (yoga, painting, functional movement, etc.) to their nationwide membership base of 5,000-10,000 senior members. Key requirement is an intuitive, user-friendly interface since their audience lacks technical skills.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Digitize in-person community classes for nationwide senior membership
Audience: Senior citizens (membership-based), currently only 5% accessing in-person classes in Mexico City
Business model: Free courses included with membership (no commerce needed)
Content types: Video classes (yoga, painting, exercise, crafts), live coaching sessions, digital downloads/PDFs
Scale expectations: 30-40 courses, 10 instructors, 5,000-10,000 student members

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Not yet established — Alejandro said "I don't have cost for this yet" but seemed receptive to $10K/yr
Authority: Alejandro is evaluating; needs to bring in Head of Experience and VP of Growth for final decision
Need: Strong — currently limited to in-person classes at one Mexico City location, company expanding nationwide
Timeline: "As soon as possible" — urgent but realistic about implementation time

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Admin or seat management: 10 instructors needing admin access, up to 15 admin users on Core plan
Special workflows or constraints: Must be extremely user-friendly for elderly non-technical users; Spanish-language support preferred for future sessions

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- "I think you are a good option to us, to be honest"
- "That sounds really good" (after live coaching demo)
- "That's excellent. That's what I was looking for" (after progress tracking demo)
- Agreed to follow-up meeting next Thursday with additional stakeholders
- Plans to bring Head of Experience and VP of Growth to next meeting

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Budget not yet allocated — needs internal approval
- Decision requires buy-in from Head of Experience and VP of Growth
- Target audience (seniors) may struggle with technology adoption regardless of platform
- Language barrier — Alejandro's English is limited, may prefer Spanish going forward

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Core Internal Training ($10,000/year, ~$833/month)
Discounts offered: Kevin indicated flexibility of "a couple hundred dollars" if pricing is an issue
Contract length discussed: Annual plan presented
Constraints or approvals needed: Budget not yet defined internally

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:
- Send email recap with plan breakdown and pricing by end of day
- Schedule follow-up meeting for Thursday (Feb 20) at same time

Customer:
- Share email recap with Head of Experience and VP of Growth
- Bring stakeholders to Thursday follow-up meeting

Scheduled:
- Next meeting date: Thursday Feb 20, 2026 (same time)
- Materials to send: Email with plan details and pricing

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
Alejandro seems genuinely interested and gave multiple positive signals throughout the demo. The fact that he's bringing VP of Growth and Head of Experience to the next call suggests real intent. Language barrier may slow communication but doesn't appear to be a blocker. Koltin is a growing company in Mexico expanding beyond Mexico City.

---

calls.title: ines gerardi and Zach McCall
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-10
ATTENDEES: Ines Gerardi (+ colleague)
COMPANY: Fundacion Flor
STAGE: Discovery / Demo

---
SUMMARY
Fundacion Flor is an Argentinian foundation that has been running in-person courses on leadership, finance, and related topics for 10 years. They want to create a virtual campus / online academy to expand their audience beyond Argentina and create a new revenue stream by selling courses online. This was a first demo call; Zach walked them through Teachable's course builder, embedding, community, coaching/webinars, organizations, quizzes, and pricing.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Create an online academy to sell existing course content (leadership, finance) to a broader audience
Audience: Current Argentinian students + new international/Latin American learners, plus B2B sales to companies
Business model: Sell courses online (one-time purchase, subscriptions, or memberships), also sell bulk seats to companies
Content types: Video courses (10-30 min segments), quizzes, possibly live webinars
Scale expectations: Starting with 1-2 courses, expanding over time

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Discussed $6,000/yr (Valentine's promo) or $5,500/yr (2-year commitment). Foundation needs internal approval for investment.
Authority: Ines and colleague are researching platforms; decision goes to their boss. They cannot commit on the call.
Need: Strong. They have 10 years of content and want to digitize and expand reach. Currently no online platform.
Timeline: Slow. They are in research phase, looking at multiple platforms. Follow-up scheduled for March 17.

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: Embedding courses into their existing website
Reporting needs: Organization-level reporting (seat usage for B2B sales)
Payments / checkout: Multi-currency support (Argentine peso), Stripe integration, flexible pricing (one-time, subscription, payment plan)
Admin or seat management: Organization seat management for B2B corporate sales
Special workflows: B2B course sales with seat allocation, community per course

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Enthusiastic about platform capabilities (embedding, organizations, quizzes, community)
- Have 10 years of content ready to adapt
- Interested in B2B sales via organizations feature
- Scheduled follow-up for March 17
- Asked for Latin American customer references

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Still in research phase, evaluating multiple platforms
- Need internal approval from boss (foundation leadership)
- Timeline is slow (March follow-up at earliest)
- Foundation investment decision involves many stakeholders and strategic discussions
- Competitive market in Argentina for online courses

---
PRODUCT FEEDBACK
- Embedding capability is important for their existing website
- Organization seat management is critical for their B2B model
- Multi-currency (Argentine peso) support is essential
- Quiz/assessment features are needed
- Want account manager guidance on content strategy (video length, course structure)

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Likely the plan starting at $6,000/yr
Discounts offered: Valentine's discount ($6,000 vs $6,500), 2-year commitment ($5,500/yr)
Contract length discussed: 1-year or 2-year
Constraints: Foundation budget approval needed

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Send recap email with platform overview and capabilities
- Provide student-side demo/experience access
- Ask product team for Latin American (ideally Argentinian) customer references

Customer:
- Internal discussions about business model and platform choice
- Communicate platform capabilities to their boss
- Start adapting content for online format

Scheduled:
- Follow-up meeting March 17, 2026

calls.title: Rob Llewellyn and Kevin Codde

CALL DATE: 2026-03-03
ATTENDEES: Robert Llewellyn, Kevin Codde (kevin.codde@teachable.com)
COMPANY: Rob Llewellyn
STAGE: Discovery

---
SUMMARY
Long-time Teachable customer (since 2015) based in Spain with a company registered in UAE. Currently using direct Stripe integration. Kevin reached out about migrating to Teachable Pay with a partnership Stripe rate. Rob also runs courses on Skool and compared their 2.9% rate.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Online course sales
Audience: Global (selling outside UAE to avoid VAT)
Business model: Course creator, multiple Stripe accounts in different countries
Scale expectations: Established creator since 2015

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Price-sensitive, comparing rates closely
Authority: Decision maker
Need: Lower processing fees, VAT flexibility
Timeline: Reviewing written offer

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- 4% rate perceived as "quite high" compared to Skool's 2.9%
- VAT handling concern for UAE-based company selling globally
- Currently on direct Stripe at ~4.2%, so margin improvement is slim

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:
- Send written offer with best rate (3.9% + $0.30 + 0% back office)
- Verify VAT setup for UAE-based seller

Customer:
- Review written offer

---

calls.title: Fadi Y and Kevin Codde
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-03-02
ATTENDEES: Fadi Y (Owner, Quebec course business), Kevin Codde (Teachable)
COMPANY: Fadi Y Course Business (Quebec, Canada - existing customer since 2016)
STAGE: Existing Customer / Retention

---
SUMMARY
Retention call with existing Teachable customer Fadi Y from Quebec, Canada (customer since 2016). Sells courses helping college students understand materials. Two churn risk factors: (1) price increases making Teachable unsustainable (~$1,100 USD/yr vs competitors at $30-40/mo), (2) limited admin/author seats causing operational headaches. Kevin offered Teachable Pay migration (2.6% + $0.30 vs current 2.9% + $0.30, plus 0% back-office fees for refunds/chargebacks/BNPL). Fadi declined to switch now due to uncertainty about staying with Teachable. Kevin to propose a package deal with pricing discount + unlimited admins. Fadi also planning a new higher-ticket online school for summer 2026.

---
USE CASE
Existing customer: Educational courses for Quebec college students. Low ticket ($19, $35, $299). Planning higher-ticket school launch summer 2026.

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin: Put together retention deal (pricing + unlimited admins), email Fadi
Fadi: Evaluate offer against competitor pricing, decide on staying vs migrating
---

calls.title: Meet with Jerome Olaloye
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-03-02
ATTENDEES: George (Founder, Equal Opportunity), Jerome Olaloye (Teachable SDR)
COMPANY: Equal Opportunity
STAGE: Discovery / Initial Inquiry

---
SUMMARY
Short SDR call with George from Equal Opportunity, who has a website for university students getting ~20,000 signups per year. He wants to offer free training on how to break into different industries (job readiness academy). Two key concerns: (1) confusion about student limits across plans (Growth plan shows 5,000 but he needs 20,000 -- requires enterprise plan), (2) custom registration fields (wants name, university, course as required fields during account creation, not a separate form). Jerome confirmed custom fields are possible, will follow up with details and schedule a platform lead call for enterprise pricing.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Free career training academy for university students
Audience: ~20,000 university students per year
Content types: Training courses on breaking into different industries
Business model: Free to students
Scale: 20,000 students/year

---
NEXT STEPS
Jerome: Send info on custom registration fields, schedule enterprise plan call
George: Confirm custom fields meet requirements before proceeding
---

calls.title: Teachable <> Speravita
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-03-02
ATTENDEES: Jennifer Roush (Director, Speravita), Kristina Edelman (Operations, Speravita), Liselle (Training Coordinator, Speravita), Zach McCall (Teachable), Lennie Zhu (Teachable Product)
COMPANY: Speravita
STAGE: Evaluation / Follow-up Demo

---
SUMMARY
Follow-up call with Speravita team (100+ centers). Lennie Zhu from Teachable product team demoed new organization admin features: center point-of-contact can enroll students (up to seat limit), view progress reports and leaderboards, download quiz score CSVs, but cannot remove students or edit org settings. Three training tracks for different user roles (learner, patient representative, etc.). Jennifer is the decision-maker with board approval. They sell seat-based training: initial fee for 3 trainees, additional trainees at separate fee. Need content by May 1 for June class. Discussed access duration per product/bundle, compliance quiz settings, and org admin permissions. Follow-up in 2 weeks (week of March 16) with pricing.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Training platform for 100+ centers with seat-based enrollment
Audience: Healthcare/professional trainees across 100+ partner centers
Content types: Courses with quizzes, CEU-style training, bundled by role track
Business model: Seat-based B2B sales to centers (initial fee + per-trainee add-ons)
Scale: 100+ centers, 3 training tracks per center

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Board approval already granted
- Jennifer is decision-maker
- May 1 deadline for content readiness (June class incoming)
- Follow-up in 2 weeks with pricing
- Already had multiple demo calls with Teachable

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach: Send pricing proposal, schedule follow-up for week of March 16
Jennifer/team: Review pricing, prepare for onboarding

---
FEATURE REQUESTS
- Organization Hierarchy (~06:00) - 100+ centers, each with own admin point of contact
- Seat Management (~14:00) - Per-center seat allocation, billing tracking when seats added
- Organization-level Reporting (~13:00) - Center-level progress tracking and leaderboards
- Course Sequencing / Completion Gating (~13:30) - Prevent quiz skipping, enforce content completion
- Access Duration / Expiration (~17:00) - Different access durations per product/bundle
- Role-based Access (~14:00) - Org admin permissions (enroll but not remove students)
---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KJQNZKMRV19Q7CR5J8WAFZQ6

ATTENDEES: Álvaro Sánchez Teruelo, Kevin Codde (kevin.codde@teachable.com)
COMPANY: Álvaro Sánchez Teruelo
STAGE: Discovery

---
SUMMARY
Existing Teachable customer flagged for exceeding user limits (16K+ users on a plan capped at 5K). Spike caused by free lead magnet courses. Álvaro plans to delete inactive/lead magnet users to stay under 5K rather than upgrade. Uses third-party financial credit entities for installment payments at 0.5% rate. Not a fit for Teachable Pay migration.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Online course sales with lead magnets
Audience: Students paying via installment credit
Business model: Course creator using freebies as lead magnets
Scale expectations: ~500 active users, 16K+ total including lead magnet signups

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Wants to stay on current plan, declined $7,200/yr upgrade
Authority: Decision maker
Need: User cleanup to stay within plan limits
Timeline: Before next week (Kevin's team deadline)

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Already has 0.5% payment processing rate, Teachable Pay cannot compete
- Reluctant to upgrade plan, prefers manual user cleanup

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin:
- Verify user count after cleanup

Customer:
- Delete inactive/lead magnet users to get under 5,000
- Email Kevin when done

---

calls.title: Connect With Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-27
ATTENDEES: Charly Gordon (Program Lead, ICC), Michelle Gutierrez (Project Coordinator, ICC), Zach McCall (Teachable)
COMPANY: International Chamber of Commerce (ICC)
STAGE: Discovery / Demo

---
SUMMARY
Discovery/demo call with the International Chamber of Commerce (not-for-profit), who have partnered with a large tech company to deliver e-commerce training for 5,000+ SME leaders across 10 markets in Latin America, Africa, and Asia. Two components: (1) self-paced online modules (3 modules, ~10 lessons each, 4-5 min video), (2) trainer-led virtual workshops. All content free to participants. Content will be 70-80% generic per region + 20-30% market-specific, in local languages. Architecture discussed: one school per country/region (10 schools). Need consolidated dashboard, white-glove onboarding, mobile-responsive experience. Also want to use platform for in-person event registration and promotion in the lead-up to module launch. Decision timeline: mid-March. Follow-up in 2 weeks.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: E-commerce training program for SME leaders in 10 emerging markets
Audience: 5,000+ small business leaders across Latin America, Africa, Asia
Content types: Short video modules (4-5 min), trainer-led virtual workshops, in-person events
Business model: Free (funded by corporate tech partner and ICC)
Scale: 10 markets/countries, 10 separate schools, multi-language

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach: Send pricing quote for 10 schools + white-glove onboarding + technical add-ons menu, check in via email
Charly/Michelle: Review quote, reconvene in 2 weeks, decision by mid-March

---
FEATURE REQUESTS
- Multi-language Support (~06:00) - Platform UI in local languages for each market
- Organization Hierarchy (~15:00) - Country-level grouping with shared + market-specific content
- Reporting Dashboard (~27:00) - Consolidated reporting across all 10 schools
- Live Sessions (~16:00) - Trainer-led virtual workshops via coaching product
- Hybrid Scheduling (~22:00) - In-person event registration and promotion via platform
---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KHY1Q8QKJ21K2T2HCD1NPHC7

marketing_data.company_description: Global not-for-profit organization supporting the business community. Partnered with a large tech company to deliver e-commerce capacity building training for 5,000+ SME leaders across 10 markets in Latin America, Africa, and Asia.
currently_evaluating: White-glove onboarding and hands-on setup support

quotes.text: We're the International Chamber of Commerce. We're a not for profit organization. And our objective is to support the global business community.
quotes.speaker: Charly Gordon

quotes.text: We're looking for a partner who can provide an e-learning platform where we can house those modules, house those trainer led workshops and that offers a super intuitive, super simple user journey for SMEs that are often super busy.
quotes.speaker: Charly Gordon

quotes.text: All the support we can get in getting this up and running, managing it would be most helpful.
quotes.speaker: Charly Gordon

terminology.standard_term: Small and Medium Enterprise business owners

calls.segment_reasoning: ICC is delivering professional e-commerce skills training for SME leaders across 10 markets, funded by a corporate tech partner. While ICC is technically a membership organization, this specific program is a professional training initiative targeting small business leaders, not a member benefit. B2B training at scale with multi-market deployment.

calls.title: Connect With Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-27
ATTENDEES: Alessandro Provera (Agency Owner, Italy), Zach McCall (Teachable)
COMPANY: Alessandro Provera Agency (Italian digital/influencer agency)
STAGE: Discovery / Active Setup

---
SUMMARY
Discovery call with Alessandro Provera who runs a digital agency in Italy working with major food influencers (~1M+ followers). They produced a 6-episode professional pizza-making course with TV production quality. Strategy: sell at high volume, low price (~59 EUR Italy, $79-89 US/UK) to build a fan base. Community: 1M Instagram, 60K emails (Klaviyo), 10K WhatsApp (managed via Charles, a Dutch platform). Architecture: custom-coded landing page -> Teachable checkout -> course delivery. Need multi-language (Italian + English), Klaviyo integration via webhooks, and API documentation. Already created a Teachable school and connected payments. Concerned about Stripe payout reliability during big launches. Evaluating Growth vs Advanced plan. Follow-up next Friday.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Sell professional food course at high volume to influencer community
Audience: Food enthusiasts, Italian market primary, expanding to US/UK
Content types: 6-episode video course (TV production quality)
Business model: One-off purchase (~59-89 EUR/USD), volume strategy
Scale: 1M Instagram, 60K email list, 10K WhatsApp community

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach: Send API documentation, recap email, check on technical help scoping
Alessandro: Analyze plans with team, review API docs, reconnect next Friday

---
FEATURE REQUESTS
- Multi-language Support (~05:00) - Italian and English versions of course
- Third-party Integration (~08:00) - Klaviyo webhook integration for email flows
- Headless / API Access (~04:00) - Custom landing page with Teachable as checkout/delivery backend
---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KJ871KCS48SJ9GR3BDF8YRW2

marketing_data.company_description: Italian digital agency working with major food influencers (1M+ followers). Produced a professional 6-episode pizza-making course with TV production quality. Strategy is high-volume, low-price sales to build fan base. Tech stack: Klaviyo (60K email), Charles (10K WhatsApp), custom-coded landing pages, paid advertising (Google/Meta).
currently_evaluating: Teachable as checkout and course delivery backend
Klaviyo integration via webhooks for email automation
Growth vs Advanced plan for API/integration needs

calls.title: ivy-xyyu-cnk
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-26
ATTENDEES: Erica Fleischer (Program Coordinator, SNAP Training Program), Kevin Codde (Teachable)
COMPANY: SNAP Job Readiness Training program (Illinois, philanthropic-funded)
STAGE: Discovery / Demo

---
SUMMARY
Demo call with Erica Fleischer about a job readiness training program for SNAP participants (able-bodied adults without dependents) in Illinois. Participants must complete 80 hours/month of work activity to maintain benefits. Program needs minimum 80 hours of job readiness training content at 8th-grade reading level with monthly reporting. Universe of 300,000 potential participants, realistic estimate ~10,000. Currently funded by a philanthropic group; state of Illinois would eventually procure. Target launch May 1st or sooner. Key gap: Erica wants platforms that come WITH pre-made content, not just a course-building tool. Rates Teachable "probably not that high" because the state already has an LMS under contract and other options include bundled content. Pro plan discussed at $10,000/year (10,000 students), scaling to ~$20,000/year at 300,000. Decision next week.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Job readiness training for SNAP participants to maintain benefits
Audience: SNAP recipients (able-bodied adults without dependents), 10,000-300,000 potential users
Content types: Videos (some from YouTube), quizzes, PDFs, resume-building exercises
Business model: Free to participants, funded by philanthropy/state government
Compliance: 80 hours/month SNAP requirement, monthly reporting by participant
Scale: 10,000 realistic start, 300,000 universe

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- Urgency: May 1st launch target, "potentially sooner"
- Actively evaluating multiple providers
- Team decision meeting tomorrow
- BUT: Erica said Teachable "probably not that high" on their list
- Competitor advantage: other LMS options include pre-made content

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin: Send written proposal for Pro package ($10K/yr) and enterprise tier ($20K/yr), check Illinois procurement partnerships
Erica: Present to team tomorrow, decision by next week

---
FEATURE REQUESTS
- Completion Tracking (~03:00) - Monthly reporting for SNAP hour requirements
- Learning Paths (~06:00) - Suggested course paths to hit 80 hours, partial credit
- File Upload & Grading (~14:00) - Student resume creation within courses
- Seat Management (~19:00) - Reassign licenses when participants drop out
---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KJDG0BDMDF4WNV4E1CS9P1KY

calls.title: Carolyn Castro and Kevin Codde
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-25
ATTENDEES: Carolyn Castro (Trinity Church), Kevin Codde (Teachable)
COMPANY: Trinity Church
STAGE: Discovery / Demo

---
SUMMARY
Discovery/demo call with Carolyn Castro from Trinity Church (staff of 30-40, ~300-400 volunteers). They want an online platform for volunteer training, discipleship tracks, and classes. Key needs: ministry-specific course organization (not all volunteers see all content), completion tracking, AI-assisted course creation, community features for online classes, and content drip. Kevin demonstrated organizations, course building, AI generation, communities, bundles, and certificates. Pricing discussed: Core internal training package at $5,000/year with 10% nonprofit discount. Carolyn needs boss approval before proceeding. Kevin offering trial link.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Online discipleship, volunteer onboarding, and training resource hub
Audience: 300-400 church volunteers across different ministries
Content types: Training videos, documents, quizzes
Business model: Free internal training (no sales intent currently)
Scale: Staff of 30-40, 20-25 admin users needed

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin: Send written proposal with trial link
Carolyn: Discuss with supervisor, potential follow-up call next week

---
FEATURE REQUESTS
- Group Enrollment (~05:00) - Ministry-specific course access
- Completion Tracking (~02:30) - Track who's watching and engagement
- Bulk User Import (~03:00) - 25 admin users creating content
- AI Course Generation (~12:00) - Help creating new courses from ideas/summaries
- Drip / Timed Content Release (~14:00) - Weekly course rollout
- Community Features (~13:00) - Discussion boards for online classes
- Content Templates (~13:00) - Faster course creation workflow
---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KJB9NSP3MS5YX6ESPV4YS0TW

calls.title: 30-minute Meeting
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-25
ATTENDEES: Trent Grooms (Director, Choices & Community Living), Dan Cochran (IT/Operations, Choices & Community Living), Kevin Codde (Teachable)
COMPANY: Choices & Community Living
STAGE: Decision / Ready to Buy

---
SUMMARY
Choices & Community Living is an Ohio nonprofit with ~275 employees (34-36% annual turnover) providing care services. They need an LMS for internal staff training across 7 positions, primarily Direct Support Professionals (DSPs). They already evaluated Teachable through a partner organization (19 Services, 9-10 year relationship) and met with Matt Horner previously. They also evaluated Study Fetch but it was too expensive and had HIPAA concerns. They plan to create 40-50 thin-slice courses (~20 min modules) with quizzes and completion gating. Need SSO integration with existing third-party software. Kevin presented Growth ($1,502/yr after nonprofit discount) vs Internal Training Core ($4,355/yr after discounts). Trent explicitly said "We're definitely going to buy" and will decide which plan within days.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Internal staff training LMS for 7 position types
Audience: ~275 employees (primarily Direct Support Professionals), ~500 user licenses
Content types: Thin-slice modules (20 min each), videos, quizzes, PowerPoints, PDFs
Business model: Free internal training now; may sell courses externally in the future
Compliance: Ohio state training hour requirements, HIPAA considerations

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- "We're definitely going to buy. So you can walk away knowing we'll buy the system."
- Already evaluated through partner org (19 Services) over 9-10 year relationship
- Narrowing between two plans, not whether to buy
- Will decide within days

---
NEXT STEPS
Kevin: Send written proposals for both plans
Prospect: Decide between Growth ($1,502/yr) and Internal Training Core ($4,355/yr), respond within days

---
FEATURE REQUESTS
- Organization Hierarchy (~05:00) - 7 position types with different training paths
- Course Sequencing / Completion Gating (~08:00) - Must pass quiz before advancing
- Drip Content (~07:00) - Thin-slice modules with progressive sections
- Quiz Question Types (~07:30) - Comprehension checks between sections
- Completion Tracking (~10:00) - Time spent, failed attempts for auditing
- Video Scrub Prevention (~11:00) - Prove video was watched for compliance hours
- AI Course Generation (~06:30) - Convert Word/PDF/PowerPoint to courses
- Third-party Integration (~09:00) - Integrate with existing care management software
- SSO / SAML (~09:30) - Single sign-on from existing system
- Reporting Data Export (~10:00) - CSV export for compliance auditing
- Certificate Automation (~10:00) - Course completion certificates
---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KJAVVH4A48DWW52M2E39BTRN

marketing_data.company_description: Ohio nonprofit providing care services with ~275 employees across 7 primary positions. Largest role is Direct Support Professionals (DSPs). 34-36% annual staff turnover. Partner with 19 Services (9-10 year relationship).
currently_evaluating: LMS for internal staff training across 7 positions
Thin-slice course modules with quizzes and gating
SSO integration with existing care management software
Compliance tracking for Ohio state training hour requirements
Growth plan ($1,502/yr) vs Internal Training Core ($4,355/yr)

calls.segment_reasoning: Nonprofit using Teachable for internal employee training across multiple positions with compliance requirements. Classic corporate education use case: training employees on organizational processes, onboarding new hires, and meeting regulatory training hour requirements.

competitor_mentions.context: Evaluated Study Fetch, a student-driven AI learning platform that generates quizzes and learning games from uploaded content. Cost prohibitive and had HIPAA data ownership concerns.

calls.title: Connect With Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-25
ATTENDEES: Keith Schneringer (Marketing Lead, Waxie), Zach McCall (Teachable)
COMPANY: Waxie (janitorial/sanitary supply company, acquired)
STAGE: Decision / Ready to Buy

---
SUMMARY
Demo call with Keith Schneringer from Waxie, a janitorial/sanitary supply company that was acquired. Keith is in charge of marketing for their Jansan customers. Their previous LMS (set up in early 2000s, had 4,000 seats) was discontinued for financial reasons. Customers keep asking for training content. Short-term: stand up a platform with 12 courses for a school district (75 custodians) by March 12 for evaluation. Mid-term: go live July 1. Long-term: expand to other customers. Needs courses, quizzes, certificates, completion tracking, organizations for B2B, white label, gamification. Zach demonstrated AI course creation, organizations, compliance settings, certificates. Premium plan discussed at $6,500/year (or $6,000/year with 2-3 year commitment). Keith wants to make a decision by Friday and start building courses next week.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Customer training LMS for custodians at school districts and companies
Audience: Custodial staff at customer organizations (first client: 75 custodians at one school district)
Content types: 12 courses initially, SOPs, icons/graphics (avoiding dated video), quizzes, job cards
Business model: Primarily value-added service; potential to monetize for deeper training content
Scale: Previous LMS had 4,000 seats; initial deployment 75 custodians, scaling to multiple clients

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- "What I'm looking to do is make a decision by tomorrow, Friday latest."
- Wants to start course creation next week
- School district deadline March 12 for evaluation, July 1 go-live
- Already spoke with Jerome (SDR) previously
- Asking detailed pricing and timeline questions
- Previous LMS discontinued, customers actively requesting training

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach: Send sandbox account and student-side demo account, recap over email, check in Friday
Keith: Evaluate sandbox, make plan decision by Friday, begin course creation next week

---
FEATURE REQUESTS
- AI Course Generation (~19:00) - Has SOPs ready, interested in AI-assisted course creation
- Organization Hierarchy (~09:00) - Multiple school districts and companies as separate organizations
- Completion Tracking (~04:00) - Supervisors assign and track custodian course completion
- Certificate Automation (~04:30) - Certificates of completion for custodians
- Quiz Question Types (~04:00) - Knowledge checks with existing question library
- White Label (~16:00) - Interested in white label app for future
- Gamification (~04:00) - Reward learning milestones
- Seat Management (~25:00) - Previous LMS had 4,000 seats, needs scalable seat model
- Custom Domain (~17:00) - Custom URL for the learning platform
- Digital Downloads (~08:00) - Job cards and product information PDFs
- Video Scrub Prevention (~04:00) - Course compliance settings for video completion
---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KJ66VSGKV5YEV2TGKVPW3Z6X

marketing_data.company_description: Janitorial/sanitary supply company (acquired). Serves custodial operations at school districts, companies, and facilities. Previously operated an LMS with 4,000 seats (early 2000s, discontinued). Has internal LMS for employee training but needs customer-facing training platform.
currently_evaluating: Customer-facing LMS to replace discontinued platform
12 courses for first school district deployment (75 custodians)
AI-assisted course creation from existing SOPs
Premium plan at $6,500/year vs self-serve options
White label app for future branding

calls.segment_reasoning: Waxie is a janitorial supply company building a customer training platform to teach custodians how to use their products. Education supports their core business (selling supplies), not IS their business. Classic customer education use case with B2B organizations, seat management, and white label needs.

calls.title: Connect With Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-25
ATTENDEES: Tobie Ashiyana (Founder, Retreat Center), Zach McCall (Teachable)
COMPANY: Tobie Ashiyana Retreat Center (Spain-based)
STAGE: Discovery / Demo

---
SUMMARY
Discovery/demo call with Tobie Ashiyana, who runs a retreat center in Spain offering live in-person retreats (e.g., "Transformation") with coaching alongside. Currently managing ~20 WhatsApp communities for retreat cohorts (growing 10 per year). Needs: coaching platform (1:1 and 1:many), community for each retreat cohort plus a central alumni community, online guide/coach training course, and a white label app. Key gaps identified: no in-app messaging for coaching (only via community DMs), no built-in calendar for coaching sessions (uses Calendly), no call logging for coach invoicing. Discussed white label app customization options (in-app messaging, calendar possible). Tobie also interested in assigning "rituals" (daily habits/behaviors) to clients via the app. Has multiple coaches on staff. Follow-up scheduled for next week after Zach checks with app development team.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Online coaching platform + community for retreat center alumni and cohort groups
Audience: Retreat attendees, coaching clients, yoga training students
Content types: Coaching sessions (1:1 video), courses (guide/coach training), community, meditations (audio/video)
Business model: Paid retreats + coaching packages + potential online course sales
Scale: ~20 community groups currently, growing 10/year; yoga training school

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach: Follow up on white label app customization questions (in-app messaging, calendar, custom features), send recap email
Tobie: Evaluate answers, reconnect next week

---
FEATURE REQUESTS
- Community Features (~03:00) - Replace WhatsApp with platform communities for retreat cohorts
- White Label (~03:30) - White label app for branded experience
- Direct Messaging (~05:00) - In-app messaging between coaches and clients
- Hybrid Scheduling (~12:00) - In-platform calendar for coaching session bookings
- Role-based Access (~20:00) - Multi-coach architecture, assigning coaches to specific clients
- Coaching Session Tracking (~20:30) - Log coaching calls for invoicing purposes
---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KJ5F37740W07JJP4PQQC9D3E

marketing_data.company_description: Spain-based retreat center offering live in-person transformation retreats with coaching. Operates yoga training school. Manages ~20 WhatsApp community groups for retreat cohorts, growing by 10 per year. Multiple coaches on staff.
currently_evaluating: Platform for coaching, community, and online courses
White label app with in-app messaging and calendar
Community solution to replace WhatsApp groups
Coach management and session tracking for invoicing

calls.title: Connect With Teachable
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-02-24
ATTENDEES: Songwoot / James (Prospect, Hong Kong/Panama), Zach McCall (Teachable)
COMPANY: Songwoot IT Services (unnamed IT services company)
STAGE: Discovery / Demo

---
SUMMARY
Discovery call with Songwoot (goes by James), who runs an IT services company based in Hong Kong for 10 years (~$2-3M annual revenue). They are relocating to Panama due to concerns about Chinese government intervention in Hong Kong financial services. The company serves primarily Japanese expatriates living worldwide and sells three products: cloud storage, collectible Japanese literature/manga, and Japanese architectural drawings. Songwoot wants to use Teachable primarily for selling Japanese literature and manga as digital content (PDFs). Zach demonstrated the platform, showed PDF hosting in course format, discussed pricing options (one-off, membership, download), and offered a sandbox account for testing. Follow-up scheduled for same time next Tuesday.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Sell Japanese literature and manga as digital content (PDFs) on a subscription/one-off basis
Audience: Japanese expatriates worldwide
Content types: PDF literature, manga, possibly architectural drawings
Business model: One-off purchases and/or annual subscriptions for digital content access
Scale: ~$2-3M annual revenue business, 10 years operating

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Payment processing: Needs Panama-compatible payment gateway (Stripe/PayPal)
Content format: PDF hosting and viewing (not page-flip, vertical scroll is acceptable)
Pricing: One-off purchase, annual subscription, and download options
Community: Interest in community features for Japanese content discussions

---
NEXT STEPS

ATTENDEES: Ben Vincent (ben.vincent@teachable.com), aidan@storylane.io, kevin.castaneda@teachable.com, jerome.olaloye@teachable.com, zach.mccall@teachable.com, anna.damico@teachable.com, efma@storylane.io, kevin.codde@teachable.com
COMPANY: Storylane

---
SUMMARY
- **Personalized Demos**: Sales can create unique demo links in under 10 seconds, enhancing tracking and engagement with prospects.

- **HubSpot Integration**: Automatically links new contacts from demos, enriching CRM data and providing real-time notifications of demo views.

- **Buyer Hub**: Allows multi-asset sharing with a single link, improving engagement and tracking activity via HubSpot.

- **Presenter vs. Creator Seats**: Clarified roles in content sharing and creation; starting trial with three to six presenter seats.

- **Trial Setup**: Initial trial will last a few weeks to a month, leveraging HubSpot and existing demo content for rapid adoption.

- **A/B Testing**: Marketing can test demo formats and gating strategies to optimize visitor engagement and lead capture efficiency.

calls.title: Teachable <> Refinery Pass
calls.hubspot_note:
CALL DATE: 2026-03-09
ATTENDEES: Rick Johnson (Refinery Pass), Kyle @RefineryPass (Refinery Pass), Zach McCall (zach.mccall@teachable.com), Lennie Zhu (Teachable)
COMPANY: Refinery Pass
STAGE: Demo / Technical Scoping

---
SUMMARY
Refinery Pass provides safety training for contractors at refineries across the US. Currently on Docebo, looking to migrate to Teachable. 50K+ student database, 60+ courses. Key challenge is managing contractor workforce that moves between companies and refineries. Need badging verification, organization-level management, and bulk purchasing. Deep technical scoping call with Lennie Zhu on organizations feature.

---
USE CASE
Primary goal: Refinery safety training and credentialing for contractors
Audience: 50K+ contractors at refineries across US (Ohio, Utah, Texas, North Dakota)
Business model: B2B training sold per-seat to contractor companies, plus individual purchases
Content types: MP4 video courses (basic refinery orientation + site-specific training)
Scale expectations: 50K+ students, 60+ products, thousands of bulk transactions per year

---
QUALIFICATION
Budget: Currently paying significantly more with Docebo. Open to 10-15K for custom development work.
Authority: Rick Johnson (decision maker), Kyle (technical lead)
Need: Migrate from Docebo. Need organization management, badging verification, bulk purchasing, AI tutor
Timeline: Ready to pull the trigger. Meeting with Maxime this week for AI tutor and custom work scoping.

---
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
Integrations: API for custom purchasing flows, AI tutor/support agent (Maxime building)
Reporting needs: Student progress lookup by name/email, completion verification for badging
Payments / checkout: Bulk seat purchasing (credit card preferred, some invoicing), flexible seat quantities
Admin or seat management: Custom roles for badging offices (read-only all students), organization admins for companies
Special workflows or constraints: Workers move between companies frequently, need credential portability. Account merging for duplicate emails. Badging offices need global student search regardless of organization.

---
BUYING SIGNALS
- "I think we're ready to pull the trigger."
- "I wish where were you guys six years ago when I needed you."
- "We're very motivated to make this work."
- Already tested sandbox account and liked it
- Open to custom development budget

---
RISKS / OBJECTIONS
- Bulk purchasing flow not yet available (Q2-Q3 roadmap)
- Organizations may be too heavyweight for small one-off contractor companies
- Workers using different emails creates identity management challenges

---
PRICING DISCUSSED
Plan discussed: Growth plan (or higher for bulk import)
Discounts offered: Significant savings vs Docebo pricing
Contract length discussed: Not specified
Constraints or approvals needed: Custom work quote from Maxime (10-15K range)

---
NEXT STEPS
Zach:
- Loop Maxime into email for AI tutor/support agent scoping
- Schedule meeting with Maxime this week
- Upgrade sandbox to include organizations feature
- Brainstorm custom bulk purchasing solution

Customer:
- Meet with Maxime for AI tutor scoping
- Continue testing sandbox

Scheduled:
- Meeting with Maxime this week (avoid Wednesday midday)

---
ADDITIONAL CONTEXT
Rick is the primary driver but Kyle handles technical side. Very engaged and motivated. Coming from Docebo which they're frustrated with (buggy SCORM, poor search, Groups feature poorly laid out). The refinery training industry sometimes uses Social Security numbers for identity which shows how critical accurate credentialing is. Rick is planning to be less involved operationally going forward.

---
TRANSCRIPT: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KJWM9VRP85A4QRAVVTBEZ39E
